This second expansion of LunaTix: Star Trackers is an anniversary expansion of 2019 to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the first Apollo landing. The game expands to actually landing on the Moon depending on your revealed mission. This can even result in an Apollo 8, 10 or 13 mission, which has other missions than landing due to the historic situation. In this game both inclination and altitude are combined with maneuvering and economic fuel usage. A way back to Earth is required to finish the game. The winner in this expansion has the most point, based on efficient maneuvering and a fast return after mission execution. Co-op in teams is encouraged in this expansion.

—description from the publisher

This is a race between a runner, an automobile and an aeroplane.
The players start at space 1 and have to reach space 40 to win the game. Movement is determined by a die. There are 3 tracks on the game board. The runners use the pink and blue spaces, the automobiles the white dots and the aeroplanes the light and dark brown spaces. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary

The perfect travelling game for any player in any occasion. Just grab a stack of tiles and keep laying them down until you hit five in a row. Easy as pie… or at least it would be. Unfortunately, there’s another player opposite you who’s trying to do the exact same thing.

Now you’ll have to worry about where they’re going and what they’re planning, all on a board with absolutely no boundaries. As you’ll soon find out it’s a game that’s very easy to learn – but tricky to win.

Try to sneak a straight or diagonal line of five or your tiles before your opponent. If you run out of tiles, you can reposition them until someone wins.

Promotional cards from PAX East 2018.

4 double-sided cards that expand Norsaga: Odds and Endings.

Immortal - Guardian: Tuva Sunwrath (Might)
Immortal - Guardian: Just Grame (Lore)
Immortal - Guardian: The Insatiable Wishard (Craft)
Immortal - Guardian: Pol at the Threshold (Faith)

Gameplay
These cards function identically to the Guardian Immortal cards found in Norsaga: Odds and Endings. When playing with Immortals, shuffle these in for additional variety.

boardgame: Battlestations: Second Edition – Advanced Rule Book
description:
You and your friends are crewmates on a starship on amazing voyages where you will fight enemy ships and space monsters, explore strange secrets, make daring rescues and escapes, solve mysteries, and save the galaxy.

This book contains the definitive complete second edition rules, compatible with all original game pieces or with the new second edition boxed set.

Includes:

60 missions including 35 inspired or designed by famous designers

30 species

140+ special abilities

12 modules

120+ pieces of personal equipment

50+ cargo bay items

150+ treasure items

100+ starship layouts
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Hexe, Tier … wer fehlt denn hier?
description:
To start Hexe, Tier … wer fehlt denn hier?, divide the cards evenly among all players, with these cards lying face up on the table in front of each player. Each card shows a young witch flying on a broom, with one or more animals accompanying her.

On a turn, one player rolls the two dice, which show various animals on them. If different animals show on the dice, then each player can turn face down all cards on which these two animals are depicted. If the same animal shows on both dice, then you have to look for only this animal on your cards. Whoever turns all their cards face down first wins the round, claiming all the face-up cards remaining in front of other players. Players then turn all their cards face up and begin a new round, which will likely favor those with fewer cards since they have less to flip over to win. At the end of three rounds, whoever has the most cards in front of them wins.

Invictus the Shogun is a 2 player tactical card game. The player will be the King and fight with opponent. While fighting with opponent’s army on the battlefield you must take care of your Kingdom because in this game, building better Kingdom than opponent’s, also let you win the game.

Destroying the enemy soldier might not always be the best move in this game. Capture them and make them your own to prosper your land.
There are multiple ways to use each card. You may use it as a soldier, as a citizen and as an assistance force. Use your cards wisely to win the battle.

2 player battle card game combined with unique mechanics such as Kingdom and Reserve, will lead you to the next Battlefield.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Cage Match!: The MMA Fight Game
description:
Cage Match! is the tabletop game of head-to-head, mixed martial arts combat. Styles makes fights!  Control a mixed martial arts fighter from a variety of backgrounds, from boxing to freestyle wrestling to Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. Defeat your opponent by knock out or submission. Secretly select your move, gain focus and then spend it on power moves. Every move has a counter and it might come down to a roll of the dice. Step into the cage!

—description from the publisher

USA On The Road is a spin-and-move game originally included in Chik-fil-a kids meals.  Players spin to move along a track that traverses the United States.  Some spaces cause players to move forward, back or lose a turn.  Other spaces ask players to do some sort of activity (e.g. pretend to water-ski or tell a story), sometimes earning an extra move or spin.  The first player to reach the Statue of Liberty wins.

On the back of the game board is one section of a five-part game called "Our Big World."

—user summary

The Path to Glory is a roll-and-move game originally published in England Activity Book, filled with various games and puzzles about the England soccer (football) team.

Players roll a die to move along a track representing a soccer game.  Some spaces represent positive or negative events in the game, and move players forward or backward or even allow a roll for an instant win.  The first player to reach the goal at the end of the track wins the game.

—user summary

Saalehkanda is a game of strategy and skill in which two opponents shoot stones (round wooden playing pieces) on a 115x70cm playing table. Saalehkanda is enjoyable for players ages 8 years and up. Playing time is approximately 10-35 minutes.
The winner of the game
is the player who first earns 50 or more points by either
       Scoring goals, 
       Pocketing the opponent‘s stones, or 
       Checking the center.
—description from the rulebook

A short quick card game in which you quickly pass around a card from you hand, until you have a set of four of the same colour. Don't get stuck with the black egg, the "faules-ei".

—user summary

We've all heard about the legends of the sirens, who with their irresistible voices lure the ships of unsuspecting sailors onto the cliffs... But did you know that they all sing in different musical styles? That's because not every sailor is attracted to the same type of music! Duh!

Every 100 years, the sirens of the salty sea wake up from their slumber, to organize the Sirenade festival. Siren clans from across the seven oceans send their most famous stars to vie for the prestigious honor of... Well, being the most badass clan!

The Sound of Sirens is a racing game with a lot of interaction between the players. Each player leads a clan of sirens, and each clan is represented by a musical genre: Pop, Ballad, Rap, and Rock.

On your turn, you will be playing action cards from your hand in order to be the first to lure your assigned ship onto the cliffs. But many surprises await along the way… Do you have what it takes to become a legendary siren?

User summary
ワードウルフ (Word Wolf) is a “werewolf”-type game from Japan. The gamemaster decides on a word and writes this word on a piece of paper to give to each player. The same word is written down for each player except one, and a similar word is written down for that one player (e.g., “taxi”, “taxi”, “taxi”, “bus”, etc.).

Each player checks the word written on their piece of paper without revealing it to the other players. The player that has the different word is the Word Wolf (but is not sure whether they are the Word Wolf).

Each player gives a short comment about their word. No lying! If someone guesses who is the Word Wolf, they win. If the Word Wolf is not revealed, the Word Wolf wins.

出題者はお題を考え、他の人に見られないようにメモ用紙に1枚に１つずつ言葉を書く。基本的に全員に同じ言葉を書くが、一人だけ「似ているけど違う言葉」を書く。（「バス」「タクシー」「タクシー」「タクシー」「タクシー」など。）
各自メモ用紙をくばられたら、自分だけこっそり見る。このとき、一人だけ違う言葉を配られたプレイヤーが今回の「ワードウルフ」となる（が、自分がワードウルフかどうかは分からない）。
順に自分の言葉についてなにか一言コメントをする。ウソをついてはいけない。
ワードウルフが誰かを当てれば勝ち。ワードウルフはばれなければ勝ち。

boardgame: プーさん ハニーコレクション (Winnie-the-Pooh Honey Collection)
description:
User summary
プーさん ハニーコレクション (Winnie-the-Pooh Honey Collection) is a Japanese game on the Disney licensed properties. In the game, you take cards from the three card piles to collect “honey”. You can take as many cards as you like on your turn.

However, if you get a “bee”, all the “honey” you collected on that turn is taken away.

Whoever collects 4 points worth of “honey” is the winner.

3つのカード山から札をめくって「はちみつ集め」をします。
一度に何枚めくってもOK!
ただし「ハチ」がでるとそのターンのはちみつは没収となります。
はやく4点分の「はちみつ集め」をできた人が「勝ち」となります。

This expansion allows Q.E. to be played with five players and includes an additional nation and an additional industry for that fifth player, along with new company tiles to cover these, as well as new "view winning bid" tokens which give players the option to see what the winning bid was in one auction of their choice during the game.

It also introduces a new scoring opportunity: "Globalization", which awards points for players investing in companies with nationalities other than their own.

boardgame: The Crucible of Classic Critters: Woodlands Dungeons for Four Against Darkness
description:
Woodlands dungeons for Four Against the Darkness for characters of level 4+

Description from the publisher:

The Crucible of Classic Critters is a supplement for Four Against Darkness offering tables, monsters, spells, magic items and special events to explore the woodlands using a new set of room and corridor tiles with elements such as trees, bridges and rivers.

Includes new rules for:

• Woodlands tables

• Animal companions and mounts

• Flying Monsters

• Subduing monsters

• Swimming (and drowning)

• A new class: the beastmaster

• Two new cleric variants

• Additional magic items and spells

• Additional monsters

40 pages, black and white illustrations.  Requires only the core Four Against Darkness book.

In Crazy Volley, you take control of a Volley team along one match. In the game, you have to sign Volley players to try to win the Volley match against other team.

The game is divided in turns: the first team that reach to win seven turns, scoring a point, win the game.
The game is composed by three kind of card:
Players, Who will be placed in field to play the match, doing their actions when the ball arrive in their field.
Referee, who can influence a turn.
Wtf card, that can change EVERYTHING!!!

But... in crazy Volley nothing is like it seems. In fact you can win the game also KILLING the players in other field...

—description from the designer

This games covers the campaign of Pyrrhus, king of Epirus, in southern Italy and Sicily to help the Greek cities threatened by the Romans and Carthaginians, from 279 to 275 BCE.

One player commands the Epirotes, Samnites, Etruscans and Greeks; the other player commands the Romans and Carthaginians.
The Campanians and Mamertines are neutral

Duration: 1 to 3 hours
    Scales: 500 to 2,000 soldiers or 10 elephants or 10 siege weapons or 10 quinqueremes.
    Modules: 1 strategic module and 1 tactical module
    1 round: 6 months

(source: rulebook)

boardgame: Lions and Tigers and Bears, Oh My!
description:
In each round of the fast-paced, dice rolling game Lions and Tigers and Bears, Oh My!, each player rolls their dice and scores based on who can boast the best beasts. Kingly lions are worth four points, tigers tally three points, and bears bring two points each. If you roll a blank, oh my!, that die is removed from play.

In more detail, on each turn, you roll your dice, then choose only one type of animal to count toward your score. Once you choose your scoring animal, you can re-roll the rest of your dice to see whether you can score more points that turn — but be careful as any blanks you get from re-rolling cause you to lose that die for the rest of the game. As soon as all players have lost all of their dice, the game ends and whoever has the most points wins.

A tower defense game set in near-future East Asia (plus USA):
Aliens are attacking the countries of the players - they appear on the top of the board and are pushed down, when more aliens appear. Aliens that reach the bottom destroy the buildings there.

Players can fight the aliens by buying military units - They destroy the aliens, which then leave a "core" that can be exchanged for money.

Your objective depends partly on your secret objective card - some gain points for helping the aliens destroy certain opponents, others have to concentrate on defense or making money. There is only one winner.

—user summary

As a boiled down version of it's bigger brother Pictionary Pocket has the same basic game play.
One player draws pictures, inspired by cards with different categories on them, and their team mates need to guess what has been drawn. If they do in a minute, they can go again, otherwise the other team has a go. Different categories score differently, whichever team reaches 10 points first, wins. Different to other versions of Pictionary, in this games a card draw decides which category is used by the player, not a die throw.

—user summary

Madeira Expansion 
Madeira will never be the same. With this modular expansion you can add new challenges to your game of Madeira. You may combine the modules in any way you choose to and personalize your gaming experience. One thing is for sure, no game will ever be the same and the challenge will increase. Do you have what it takes?

Module #1 New Beginning - 24 Starting Tiles
Module #2 Capitanias - 24 Capitania Cards
Module #3 King's Demands - 30 Unique Crown's Requests 
Module #4 New Roles - 5 New Characters, Pirate Meeple, Pirate Ship, Corsair Die, 10 special tiles

--original publisher's description

A Components: Looney Pyramid Games spaceship maneuvering game.

You will need 36 Looney Pyramids - 3 trios each of 4 colors (Red, Green, Blue, Yellow), a Pyramid Die, and a Starship Captain tin (or similar circular object, like a coaster or a CD).

Colonial ships move by distorting space-time and reappearing at the outer edge of a warp field. After they move, they fire energy beams that teleport other differently-colored ships far across the galaxy. Your mission: harness these warp energy beams to transport a diverse collection of ships, and be the first to form a complete colony!

You and your opponents move ships around the play area via the “warp” using the Starship Captain tin lid to measure distance and direction of travel. After a ship warps, it fires its teleportation beam at another target ship. You roll the pyramid die to find out if the beam successfully teleports the target ship out of the play area and into your own colony.
The goal is to be the first to teleport a combination of ships into your colony: The first player to colonize either two monochrome trios of ships or two ships of every color, wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tiny Epic Zombies: Kickstarter Deluxe Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack includes the Deluxe content offered for Tiny Epic Zombies during its Kickstarter.

This promo pack includes:

4 playable characters - the Burglar, the Lawyer, the Farmer and the Trucker.

5 search cards - the Service Dog, the Katana, the Bazooka and 2 Ninja Stars.

2 ITEMeeple items - the Katana and the Bazooka

1 wood Dog meeple

Brown Chicken, Brown Cow

“Bow Chicka Bow Wow”
The funky signature sound of golden age adult movies now lives on in popular culture as “Brown Chicken, Brown Cow”. Innuendo for “Getting Down”

Now get ready to get your hands a little dirty.

A hilarious Party Game that plays in fast and fun 10 minute rounds!
Easy to learn and easy to play.
No skill required – just have some fun! The Sculpting Game Of Naughty Innuendo
Fast & Easy! Naughty & Fun!
Get your hands dirty with this hilarious party game that is easy to learn and play.
Sculpt clean versions of dirty words then guess who’s sculptures match which words to score
2 - 6 Players, Ages 17+

HOW TO PLAY
Draw an Innuendo Card.    
You have 30 seconds to sculpt a clean version of the dirty word.

Vote on which sculptures match which Innuendo Cards.
Draw
Draw an Innuendo Card.

Sculpt
You have 30 seconds to sculpt a clean version of the dirty word.

Mix
Mix up and reveal everyone’s Innuendos.

Vote

The Order of the Purple Sky is an order of sorcerers and sorcerers in Westarland.  All the chapters of the order are looking forward to the great gathering next year to celebrate the passing of the founder of the Order.  You are a member of the Binnery chapter, one of the smaller chapters of the order. You and your colleagues are anxious to make a big impression in the competitions and demonstrations.  To that end, you are competing with your sorcerer and sorceress brothers and sisters for the chance to represent your chapter at the coming Jubilee at the Order's headquarters in Pluridan.

Everyone will be cooking up new spells, practicing their best old ones, and competing like crazy people for the best slots on the jubilee program.

Mystical Conjuration is a pattern building worker placement game for 2-5 players.  It plays in about 20-25 minutes per player.

In Mystical Conjuration, you use your sorceror and familiars to collect mana from the Astral Planes, the Aether, the Maelstrom, and even the Nether.  Combine these according to different formulas on your spell forge to complete spells and earn victory points at the same time as earning rewards such as additional familiars, runes, and glyphs to help in your spell crafting.

The mana you collect can be used to complete formulas or to take actions in the process of making new spells.  Your sorceror and familiars can collect mana or manipulate mana or formulas.

Actions include acquiring new spell formulas, moving or swapping mana between magic circles, adding mana to the formula on your spell forges, and moving spell formulas between forges.

Will you be the sorceror with the most prestige and be send to represent your chapter at the Order of the Purple Sky Jubilee?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: Build Time Machine Promo Card
description: Become a lord or time and space in your games of Pursuit of Happiness with the Build Time Machine promo card!

Welcome to Mort-A-Mania, the knock-down, drag-out, fight-night clash! Take on the role of necrosurgeon as you build your fighter out of the animated remains of last week’s toughest contenders. Purchase tumultuous tonics, scintillating spellbooks, and battle-ready body parts at auction using your own life; who needed those last 10 years anyway? Set your monstrosities against one another in a free-for-all winner-take-all combat to the undeath!

Mort-A-Mania is a quick-play beat’em up card game that’s approachable to all levels with an emphasis on player interaction. Spellbooks, tonics, and body parts keep the combat engaging and visceral. Perfect for a quick match or an all-night tournament, Mort-A-Mania promises to provide perpetual play and politics in practically precise proportions.
 It’s Magnificent! It’s Mayhem! It’s…

Mort-A-Mania!

Mort-A-Mania is played in rounds, each round beginning with an auction. Cards are dealt onto the table for all players to see, then each player secretly bids on each card. When all players are ready, bids are revealed and the highest bidder on each card wins it and pays the bid out of their life pool.

After the Auction comes the Combat, where players take turns using their newly purchased limbs, spells and tonics to deal damage to their opponents. After everyone has taken their actions and the dust settles, damage is tallied up, dead players are eliminated, and the game moves to the next round.

When only one player left alive, they are declared that winner!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Dread Streets: The Cinematic Swashbuckling Game
description:
As far as the eye can see, it stretches on and on, the endless cityscape known only as the Dread Streets. In the afternoon, true to form, the Commoners bar their doors and windows. For the Heroes of Dread Streets are about to awake from their drunken sleep, to beat each other up in yet another senseless brawl!

Dread Streets lets you direct a swashbuckling movie, complete with ridiculous stunts, pirates, musketeers, freaks, drinking, brawling and lots of taunting.

After having toured conventions in Denmark since 2013 we’re finally bringing you the complete Dread Streets game as a printer-friendly PDF, complete with printable miniatures, cards and tokens.

FIRST YOU ROLL – THEN YOU PLAN! Your turn won’t be ruined by bad luck. You roll the dice before you decide what to do, then you plan accordingly. Experience humble success instead of epic fiasco!
MULTIPLAYER. Three's not a crowd with Dread Streets. It plays equally well with 2, 3 or 4 players.
COMPATIBLE. Dread Streets is designed with 25-28mm miniatures in mind, but our Zone system lets you use any size of miniature and/or battle map.
NO MEASURING. The play area is defined by the placement of buildings and terrain, not by strictly measured distances.
COMPLETE … SORT OF. The game comes with printable miniatures, cards and tokens. You only need to supply a battle map, terrain, snacks and booze!

—description from the publisher:

“Frog Soup”, a cooperative game of jumping frogs.

The Witch is cooking a frog soup, and you are the last ingredients to the recipe. So, cooperate, jump and try to spill the soup before she puts you in the cauldron.

Frog Soup is a cooperative skill game where you will have to hit big targets on the table with your jumping frogs.

Try to hit all the magical items in the correct sequence to remove the magical protections of the Witch and escape! 
Beware! The Witch adds one ingredient per round in her cauldron. If she finishes her recipe (6 turns), everybody loses!

Children and adults alike will have a lot of fun in play this exciting skill cooperative game. 
“Frog Soup” is a game for 1 to 4 players, from 5 years old.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Arkham Horror (Third Edition): Arkham Nights 2018 Promotional Character Card Set
description:
One new, official, Investigator, given to attendees of the Arkham Nights event held on 19-20 October 2018.

Consists of the following items:

“Ashcan” Pete Investigator Sheet
“Ashcan” Pete Investigator Token
Dark Dreams Talent Card
Duke Ally Card
Pete’s Guitar Item Card

Mostly the same, a slightly different "Ashcan" Pete is included in the Expansion Under Dark Waves. Instead of the Dark Dreams talent card he ha Wanderer.
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Fill your beehive with nectar, pollen and bees to survive the coming winter in this uniquely strategic Euro-style game about bees and the natural environment.

Raise forager bees to collect nectar & guard your hive, nurse bees to care for your babies & raise male drone bees to eat other players honey. The aim is to collect (or rob!) as much nectar as you can before winter arrives, while avoiding bears, beekeepers and other pests.

This game is designed to play as competitively or cooperatively as you choose, when playing with children you can choose to not rob their hives and hold onto your cards, or you may like to target the older players, those who are getting ahead, or your husband / wife.

There are 3 ways to play the game:
1. Child’s Play 15-30min
 For younger children, 6-10yrs 
A simpler version of the game without drones, complicated dice rules etc 

2. Standard Game  60-90min
Adults and kids 10yrs+, play until the first beehive is full.
Good for beginners and introduces strategy. Drones are rarely used .

3. Extended Game 90-120min
Adults and kids 12yrs+, play until all nectar & pollen taken from board.
Much more strategic, drones are used extensively to steal from each others hives

A aesthetically pleasing game which focuses on touch and feel, with wooden hexagon laser-cut tiles, printed cotton cloth for the board and unique clay bees.

Rules closely mimic how a bee colony actually works and players naturally learn bee ‘rules’ such as how far bees will forage (5km), using the waggle dance to tell other bees where the best nectar is located (waggle wheel spinner), and rob weaker hives in autumn (hero or hazard cards).

Pathogens are attacking the population of the city! As good healthcare professionals you have the mission of thwarting this threat. Your city depends on you! Micro-Combat is a game of cooperation - you can only overcome these pathogens by working together!

Micro-Combat is a game designed to work among the young population on the subject of infectious diseases and to understanding the causes and risk factors for antimicrobial resistance.

In Micro-Combat you will play the role of health authorities who have the mission of thwarting the attack of pathogenic agents (virus, bacteria, fungi and parasites) which can make the population of your city ill. Your goal is to avoid any of the characters in the game losing all their defenses, and to do this you will have different preventive measures and medications... which are not always enough.

The game has 3 types of cards: character cards, attack cards (pathogens, "out of stock" cards and antimicrobial resistance cards) and defense cards (prevention measures and medications)
At the start of the game, 5 characters are chosen at random and placed in the center of the table. Each character has some defense points and an innate prevention measure.

The game consists in using the defense cards in order to stop the characters from losing their defense points due to the effects of the pathogen cards. Everyone plays at the same time, with no turns or player order. The pathogens are defeated through player cooperation. The game consists of 6 rounds. Each round includes the following phases: exposure (to pathogens); prevention and treatment; infection and contagion. If a character reaches zero defense points, the players lose the game.

-description from designer

Ganymede: Moon enlarges each player board with a new location on both the left (the Moon) and the right (the Ganymede Council Chamber). You can transport councilors to this Council Chamber, with sixteen councilors in the game, each with a unique ability available only to the player who holds it. One such councilor, Marty Simon, allows you to choose an Earth shuttle from those available and place it in the column under your own board without triggering its effect or moving settlers.

Each councilor is worth victory points — 1 VP in the case of Marty Simon —and each player can claim four during play, with the last one acquired always granting a powerful bonus...

Easy come, cheesy go!
Stop those mice! Use the transparent cards to cover the mice and collect cheese tokens, but remember that this game may serve you more than one unexpected course of cheesy surprises!
Contents: 4 game board parts + 1 game board frame, 55 cheese cards, 24 plastic shells + 24 cheese discs, 20 paw tokens, sand timer, stickers, rules.

—description from the box

Cheese, Please
The mice are scuttling around on a big block of cheese and it’s your job to cover them before the ravenous cat catches them in the act. Try to cover the mice with the cheese cards, but make sure the whole mouse is hidden!

     Comes with transparent plastic cheese cards!
     The game board can be changed to make the game more versatile.


—description from the publisher
PitchCar Extension: The Upsilon consists of two Y-shaped track components that split one track into two or combine two tracks into one. Use them as you wish!

PitchCar Extension: The Adapter is sized for PitchCar on one end and PitchCar Mini on the other. Two track components are included so that you can create a loop that transitions from one size track to the other, then back again.

Monopoly Cash Grab is a mad dash to get the cash! To play, load the money into the money blaster (a.k.a., the bank), then spin the spinner to blast loads of cash and Chance cards into the air. Rush to grab as much cash as you can to win the game — but don't stop there! Collect Chance cards to add more strategy to the game, such as swapping money stacks with another player, taking half of any one player's money stack, or making all players pass their money stack to the left. Once the blaster is empty, the player with the most cash wins!

—description from the publisher

Battle powerful enemies like the gelatinous Bohmerk, mindless creatures that become more powerful with each turn, and the crafty Dhargon, who collect resources to multiply their numbers!

Explore many more locations that profice branching adventures, unfolding through the choices you make in the Vile Invaders Guide!

The Vile Invaders expansion includes 9 new Heroic Quests, 12 new Side Quests, and over 70 new cards, tokens, and instructions that add a new mechanic: crafting.

Crafting materials can be used to upgrade items and make new ones!

boardgame: MON: Anarchy Phantasy Multi Card Game
description:
A card deck that can be used in many ways. Can even be used as dice.

54 cards + 2 rule cards forms a deck of 56 cards. The deck can be used as normal playing cards from 2 to ace + jokers. But also as a Japanese Hanafuda deck (48 cards + 6 jokers). The cards can be used playing Trumps, Dingo, Old Maid and other games also.

You can also battle with the characters. Pick five random cards each and do not show your opponent. The rest of the cards is placed in a “dice pile”. The player who most recently was in a fight (for real!) start. It might be back in preschool? Pick one of your characters and decide if you want to do a physical attack; the sword or a mental attack; the candle. Sword attacks are obviously defended by the shield and candle attacks are defended by the smiley face; humour.

The opponent picks out a character he thinks can defend without knowing the attacking value. Now both player draws a card from the dice pile and add the eyes on the card's die to their value, starting with the attacker. Highest total wins and is placed in front of the winner as a used character that won the battle. Also place the die card under it, it's the price for winning; a new recruit. The defeated character is placed in bottom of the dice pile (face up) and the die card (face down).

For example: player one (Kajsa) gets Lonar, Cadran, Minx, Dag and Moog. She chooses Lonar, one of the most powerful characters in the game and attacks with brains/candle (value 8). Player two (Kalle) chooses to defend with his character with highest humour, but that is still only Silver (5). Kajsa takes the first card in the dice pile which is Ulg. Ulg has however only 1 eye on his T6. 8 + 1 = 9 in mental attack. Kalle draws the next card and gets Donolen who has 5 eyes on the T6. 5 + 5 = 10. Silver wins and is placed in front of Kalle along with Donolen. They are now battle weary and not available until everybody have fought. Kajsa's Lonar is discarded under the dice pile along with Ulg. But defeated cards like Lonar can return as an undead and is placed face up! Ulg is placed face down like all other cards. Kalle wins the battle and starts the next round.

If the numbers are even the result is a tie, place both characters in front of the players, they are also battle weary and not available. However the dice cards are discarded and not won. Continue battling until all characters have fought. If both players have character cards left after everybody has fought, pick them up again and repeat everything. Shuffle the dice pile again and remember to keep the undead face up. The one with the least cards start the next battle. If even use rock-papers-scissors to decide. If an undead gets into your army keep it upside down on your hand. When played it automatically defeats both the opponent and him/herself. Remove the undead from the game after battle. The loser is the player who lost all of his/her cards. If one player has more characters to play than the other those are rested until next round.

Try adding rules like that darker cards have +1 against lighter cards or that same silhouettes can’t fight each other for example.

—description from the publisher

description:
The Deluxe edition is a compilation of the base game and Tiny Epic Zombies: Kickstarter Deluxe Promo Pack.

GENERAL INFO: 1-5 players, 30 minutes, 5 play modes including competitive and cooperative play.


     Cooperative team-play with game controlled Zombies.
     Cooperative team-play with Zombies controlled by a non-team player.
     Competitive free-for-all with game controlled Zombies.
     Competitive free-for-all with Zombies controlled by a player.
     Solo.


THEME: Modern day Zombie outbreak at the Echo Ridge Mall.

HOOK: Fast paced, portable, and tons of replayability. Play as the survivors or the Zombies. Plus ITEMeeples!

WIN: Survivors must complete 3 objectives - Zombies must kill all the Survivors or gain control of the Mall's Courtyard.

OVERVIEW: In Tiny Epic Zombies, survivors are always on the run, collecting weapons, killing Zombies and working toward completing objectives. Completing 3 objectives can win you the game but if you're too focused on the objectives, the Zombies will overrun the mall and that will be the end of you.

Based on the mode of play, you will either play as one of the Survivors or as the Zombies.

As a Survivor, your turn will consist of moving 3 times. Then based on where you move, you'll be able to do additional things. For instance, if you move into a room with a Zombie, you'll have to fight it by rolling the Zombie die. Or if you move into a room adjacent to a Zombie, you can spend ammo to shoot it. Killing a Zombie is actually pretty easy... but they just keep coming. Keeping them from overrunning the entire mall, while trying to accomplish your objectives, that is hard!

After you've taken your 3 moves, you'll search the room. This is done by revealing the Search Card you are holding. Most of the time, you find something useful, like awesome items you can later pickup and equip to your ITEMeeples. Sometimes though, you may find a Zombie lurking in the corner. So be prepared.
After you search the room, more Zombies flood into the Mall. Then it's the next player's turn. Simple as that.

Note: If your ammo and wound tokens ever meet, you're dead meat! So, keep your wounds low and your ammo high.

If you die, that character will turn into a Zombie and you'll have to draw a new character to play. If that happens too many times, you lose the game.

As a Zombie player, not only will you control unique Zombies with special abilities, you will control ALL the Zombies! With your help, the Zombies will have a brain in addition to eating brains. Your game will be to overwhelm the players with clever placement and movement of the Zombies. Smother them with your Zombie horde and prevent them from completing their objectives. If you kill enough players or overrun the mall courtyard, you win.

boardgame: ATS To The Volga II: Mamayev Kurgan
description:
MAMAEV KURGAN, STALINGRAD, RUSSIA, 27 September 1942: Acknowledging the damage done to his army in assaulting the southern and central sections of the city, General von Paulus had systematically reconfigured his forces for a fresh assault. Two shock groups were formed, with one consisting primarily of the 100th Jäger Division, whose task was to clear and control Mamaev Kurgan, followed by an advance into the Tennis Racket and the Bannyi Ravine and finally to occupy the Krasnyi Oktiabr workers' settlements. Paulus' counterpart, General Chuikov was well aware of these preparations and had planned appropriate spoiling attacks, though he was unaware of the fresh German reinforcements. His attack was disrupted before it could even begin, with waves of Stukas strafing his advancing forces. His strongpoint on the crest of the burial mound was destroyed. Bombs and artillery cut down the attackers, decimating their ranks and allowing the following German advance to gain ground. The jägers advanced two regiments abreast and captured the western slopes, but initially failed to take the southern slopes. The 227th Jäger Regiment's vigorous assaults, supported by intense artillery, destroyed the Russian ability to hold on. The Germans would secure the summit that evening (though some bypassed remnants would harass the Germans on the southern and western slopes for the next few days). By day's end, the 95th Division retained a foothold on the northern and eastern slopes of the burial mound, but was down to only 400 effective fighting men.

This is the Advanced Tobruk System treatment of this bloody battle which includes a large-hex map on heavy cardstock panels, new overhead (OVR) infantry and light weapons counters and the usual set of scenarios, Battlefield Walkaround document and AFV cards.
Ownership of ATS Rules, playaids, system markers and a "standard" Russian/German sheet of AFV counters are required to make use of this product.

—description from the publisher

When Kurt Student focused his attention on Unternehmen Merkur, the stage was set to introduce the world to the airfield at Heraklion, Crete. The defenders included British, Australian and Greek soldiers. The write up for the book by by Yannis Prekatsounakis says succinctly, “The Battle for Heraklion - an epic struggle - remained largely forgotten and widely unstudied. Yet the desperate fight for Heraklion had everything: street-fighting in the town; heroic attacks against well-fortified positions and medieval walls; heavy losses on all sides…"

Contents:
- Six 11x16" map panels depicting the Heraklion airfield and surrounding area
- Five half-sheets of counters providing all of the combat counters required
- Eight new scenarios
- Battlefield Walkaround
- One AFV card (British Matilda II)

Players are required to have the ATS Rules, playaids and generic system markers to make full use of this product.

—description from the publisher

First you get the chickens... then you get the eggs... then you get the coins! Jump start your chicken farm and sell loads of eggs in this card game for 2-6 players. Make your chickens extra happy with upgrades like Disco Speakers and Autofeeders. Happy chickens lay more eggs, and the first player to 15 coins wins!

Turn Sequence:

     Calculate all upgrades, then collect an Egg card for each of your Chicken cards.
     Draw 1 Action card.
     Take any 3 Actions. You may use the same Action multiple times.
     At the end of your turn, if you have more Eggs than you have space for in Egg Cartons, you must discard extra Egg cards.


Actions:

     Draw an Action card.
     Use an Action card.
     Buy a Chicken card.
     Buy an Upgrade card.
     Draw an Egg card.

The first player to collect 15 coins wins!

—description from the publisher

Three cards with pictures on them are dealt. You pick one card and write down the name of what's written on the card, then everyone reveals what they wrote at once. If you gave the same answer as someone else you get points. It sounds simple but it's surprisingly difficult!

For example, if you see brown liquid in a cup with steam coming off it, is that coffee, tea, cocoa, or something else?

There are also further complications. You get extra points for matching the dealer most of the time, so you have to think about what they'll write. If all the background colors match, matching the dealer doesn't give you an advantage, but all points are tripled! Some cards have a star on them that means points are doubled, but they might be harder to give a single name to than the other cards...

The rules are in Japanese and many of the cards are chosen with relevance to Japanese culture or language, but you don't need to read or understand Japanese to use them. (Example: there is a picture of a snail. This has only one obvious name in English, but in Japanese there are a number of words for snail varying by region.)

—user summary

description:
Road Trip is a spin and move game with a modular board.  It was originally a giveaway at McDonald's restaurants.

The game consists of 20 punch out paper tiles printed with road patterns.  Players arrange the tiles to create a town, then spin the paper spinner to move their paper cars around the board, trying to make it from start to finish.  The spinner determines the direction of movement rather than the distance.  All moves are one square.  Spins may also move players to one of two traffic circles or allow movement of another player's piece.

Goal:
You have to rebuild the chameleons on the table piece by piece, and try not to be the colour with most points on any of them.

At the end of a round, Player which colour has the most points on a chameleon will score these points. Players may score on several chameleons at once.

As soon as a player reaches 32 points, the game is over and the player with the least points win.

Overview:
A full game takes places in several rounds, a round is made of 6 turns, a turn is made of 3 phases

1. Picking cards
Each player secretly selects the card he wants to play and puts it face down on the table.

2. Throwing dices
When each player has a face down card in front of him, everyone roll their dice. The player with the lowest score starts. In case of a tie, tied players reroll their dice until a winner comes out.

3. Playing card
First player reveals his card and plays it. Then, starting with the player on his left, the other players do the same in turns clockwise.

Once the construction of a chameleon has started, you ought to complete its construction placing cards on it clockwise.

When all players have played, start the next turn, starting back at phase 1.

—user summary

Promo card that was given for free as part of the Pocket Dragon Kickstarter-campaign. The Card can now be bought from their Offical Webshop as part of the "Promo Pack"

Setup: Place this card next to the main board, within reach of all players.

Its action: The player may remove all Free Action markers from their player board (including from buildings and superprojects on their player board), making the Free Actions (and the markers) available again. Additionally, power up an exosuit at no cost (if able), and score 1 victory point.
Any worker may be placed here without restriction. Only a single exosuit may be present here at any given time.

When playing against the Chronobot solo opponent, the Chronobot takes this location (but still gaining 2 water and scoring 1 victory point) the first time per round it is unable to take any other action.

Promo card that was given for free as part of the Pocket Dragon Kickstarter-campaign.

Setup: Place this card next to the main board, within reach of all players.

Gameplay: This is a new location to use during the Place Characters phase. A single character can be assigned here (that gains +1 Action Point), by using any Workshop assignment card (including Special Assignment cards). Expert Preparations - for each spent AP, the player may place one Trick Marker on one of their Tricks (where component requirement is met), regardless of the AP cost of that Trick’s preparation, or whether there are Trick Markers already on that trick.

Promo card that was given for free as part of the Pocket Dragon Kickstarter-campaign.

Gameplay: Married Bliss can be acquired like any other Technology, for 3 research icons. At the end of the game (besides its printed 3 VP) it scores 1 point for every pair of tents the player has on a tile. Houses do not count, and two tents on two different tiles do not count as a pair. (Four tents on a single tile however count as two pairs.)

boardgame: Kitchen Rush: Piece of Cake – The Wedding Cake
description:
Promo card given for free as part of the Pocket Dragon Kickstarter-campaign.

Setup: Place this card next to the deck of orders, if the players agree to take the Wedding Cake Challenge.

Gameplay: Any player one can take this card without a Waiter action (and get the plate for it). The first layer of the cake has to be completed by the end of the round in order to progress in the Wedding Cake Challenge. If unfinished, do not lose prestige, etc. If finished, return the card to its original place along with the plate and ingredients that completed the first layer. During the following round(s) any player (including the same or different one than the one that made the first layer) may take the card and the previous layer to make the next layer. The next layer’s plate has to be placed on top of the first layer, and moved together. When the second layer is finished, repeat the procedure for the third layer as well. Note, in a single round only a single layer can be made.

Once all 3 layers have been completed (which can be at the end of the third or fourth round), players gain 10 money and 3 prestige. If the Wedding Cake Challenge is not completed by the end of the fourth round, the game ends in defeat. This is in addition to the normal victory conditions.

1812 is the third game of Conflict Simulations LLC's 2140 series, small-footprint wargames that play within 2 hours and no more than 140 counters. It covers the French invasion of Russia in 1812 and their attempt to force a political collapse against the Tsar.

Units are infantry corps and cavalry divisions, who each belong to one of their nation's Army Groups. Army groups are led by leaders who generate Command Points each turn, used to order units under their command.

French supply is limited, the player must manage this limited resource throughout the whole campaign in order to succeed. The Russian player must carefully manage a defensive campaign while keeping the pressure on Poland and Prussia. The Russian player must also manage national morale as to not demoralize the population by burning down cities and the countryside, as was done historically.

Combat has players rolling 1-3d6 depending on the number of Strength Points being used in combat. Both sides must manage Attrition as to make sure not to whittle away their forces needlessly. The game covers the drive on Moscow from late June through the end of December of 1812. Prussian and Austrian units are included as they were present historically.

Each hex is approximately 25 miles. Each turn covers 2 weeks and each Strength Point is the equivalent of 10,000 men and their accompanying artillery batteries.

WEIGHT: 4/10
SOLITAIRE: 7/10
PLAYERS: 1-2

boardgame: Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood of Venice – Creed Versus Crows
description:
The Competitive Versus Game Mode is played with 2 to 4 players.

Teams start from opposed sides of the game board and must complete objectives before the other team. Both teams can call on their Mentor (Ezio Auditore or Cesare Borgia) to come help them.

Objectives are variable and range from destroying the other team’s Tower to capturing an Objective in the other side’s territory, to eliminating all the opponents’ team and more. The Assassins and Crows are intruders in the Fortress so Guards will act as usual against both teams.

Murder Mystery party game that you host at home for 8-12 players who dress up as their characters. One of them is the murderer and the others must figure out who it is by studying the clues, police report, map, crime scene photos, and cross examining the other players.

It’s early century, but the rapid development of steam technology makes it feel like we are living in the far future. Steam power has ushered in a technological age with machines and devices that are amazing. Steam power is modern power, yet the age old drives of greed, lust, envy, and hate still guide us, and still, murder happens.

The guests in this steampunk world arrive at the Lilly Hotel to gather in this quaint place in the middle of town, yet surrounded by woods. Actually, that sounds a little creepy. It’s a comfortable establishment with good food, but the Lilly’s claim to fame are the seances held there. So ghosts are expected, as are the lost loved ones. Perhaps what is surprising are all the tawdry foibles of the living; greed, secrets, lust, and the soon to be dearly departed.

So sit down at the table and as soon as the honored guest arrives by steam driven airship you can be assured of an interesting evening… and that someone ain’t gonna make it.

It's not enough to think for your opponent
In this game you BECOME your opponent!

Objective: Be the first to form a straight line of 4 pawn in a row (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) in your chosen color.

     First, players choose either Red or White for their pawn color.
     Each player gets 7 playing pieces , each consisting of a red pawn and a white pawn connected by an elastic band.
     Taking turns, players add 1 playing piece to the board, and stretch the band accordingly. Note: Players place both their own color and that of their opponent in the same turn.
     The first player to form a straight line of 4 pawns in his or her color wins the game!
     All 14 playing pieces placed with no winner? Player then take turns moving their own pawns until someone wins!

"La Sfida agli 8000" is an educational game about safety, equipment, etiquette and more about the world of mountain hiking. The game is co-designed by Marco Confortola, a professional Italian climber who already conquered ten "eight-thousanders".

Players compete in two or more teams. Teams win by climbing and conquering all of the fourteen eight-thousanders and to do so, they need the correct equipment. Equipment is won by correctly answering questions about the word of hiking, trivia style.

—description from the publisher

"Kiev 1943" is a wargame by Nicolas Stratigos, the third game of the "Storms in the East" VaeVictis jeux d'histoire series.

After the failure of Zitadelle in mid-July 1943, then the loss of Kharkov on 23 August, the German armies surged back on the Dnieper with the Soviet forces at their heels. The Voronezh Front of Vatutin had the mission of charging on Kiev and destroying the 4. Panzerarmee.
On 21 September, the 3rd Guards Tank Army reached the Dnieper and established a bridgehead at Bukrin, near Kanev, soon joined by the 27th and 70th Armies. On 26 September, the Soviets dropped two paratrooper brigades on Kanev. The operation was a disaster. Meanwhile, the 38th Army forced the river at Lyutezh, 20 km north of Kiev.
The Soviets launched another operation from Bukrin mid-October with no result. The Stavka realised the tactical impasse of this bridgehead and decided – in absolute secrecy – to transfer Rybalko’s 3rd Guards Tank Army into the pocket of Lyutezh.
Vatutin prepared a large-scale attack on Kiev. On 3 November, more than 2000 canons discharged a flood of fire on the units of the VII. and XIII. Korps; the 68th and 88th infantry divisions were impotent against the 38th Army supported by Kravchenko’s 5th Guards Tank Corps. Farther north, the 60th Army attacked in the direction of Korosten.
Despite the counter-attacks of the 7. and 8. Panzer and of the 20th Pz.Grenadier, Rybalko’s men entered Kiev and completely liberated it on 6 November. Fastov fell on 7 November, and the Soviet troops advanced on the entire Front. Jitomir fell at the hands of the 1st Guards Cavalry Corps on 12 November. The Soviets advanced more than 120 km to the west!
The Germans sent reinforcements from everywhere. The 25. Panzer somehow slowed down the Soviets at Fastov with numerous losses; the 1. SS, the 2. SS and the 1. Pz Divisions blocked the Soviet advance in the south.
On 13 November, Von Manstein launched a counter offensive with the 48. PzKorps toward Jitomir and Brusilov in the southern flank of the Soviet penetration.
As a Soviet, will you be able to liberate Kiev for the anniversary of the Bolshevik Revolution ? As a German, will you restore the front of the 4. Panzerarmee ?

Game scale:
A game turn represents two days of real time.
One hex covers approximately 6 km.
The units represent divisions, brigades, regiments, KGs, battalions or HQs, with their organic elements.

—description from the publisher

Un jeu de Nicolas Stratigos dans la collection VaeVictis jeu d'histoire.
"Kiev 1943", est le 3e volume de la série "Orage à l'Est".

Après l’échec de Zitadelle mi-juillet 1943 puis la perte de Kharkov le 23 août, les armées allemandes refluent sur le Dniepr, talonnées par les forces soviétiques. Le front de Voronezh de Vatutin a pour mission de foncer sur Kiev et détruire la 4. Panzerarmee.
Le 21 septembre, la 3e armée de chars de la Garde atteint le Dniepr et établit une tête de pont à Boukrine, près de Kanev, bientôt rejoint par les 27e et 70e armées. Le 26 septembre, les Soviétiques larguent deux brigades de parachutistes sur Kanev mais l’opération tourne au désastre. Pendant ce temps, la 38e armée force le fleuve à Lyutezh, 20 km au nord de Kiev. Le 30 septembre, Le groupe d’armées Sud a enfin franchi le Dniepr. Mi-octobre, les Soviétiques lancent de nouveau une opération à partir de Boukrine mais sans résultat. Se  rendant compte de l’impasse tactique de cette tête de pont, la Stavka décide le transfert – dans le plus grand secret – de la 3e armée blindée de la Garde de Rybalko dans la poche de Lyutezh.
Vatutin prépare alors une attaque d’envergure sur Kiev. Le 3 novembre, plus de 2 000 canons déversent un déluge de feu sur les unités des VII. et XIII. Korps, Les 68. et 88. ID ne peuvent rien face à la 38e armée soutenue par le 5e corps de chars de la Garde. Plus au nord la 60e armée attaque en direction de Korosten.
Malgé les contre-attaques des 7.et 8. Panzer et de la 20. Pz.Grenadier, les hommes de Rybalko entrent dans Kiev et la libère totalement le 6 novembre. Fastov tombe le 7 novembre, les troupes soviétiques avancent sur tout le front. Jitomir tombe aux mains du  1er corps de cavalerie de la Garde le 12 novembre. Les Soviétiques ont alors avancé de plus de 120 km à l’ouest ! Les Allemands envoient des renforts de partout. La 25. Panzer ralentit tant bien que mal les Soviétiques à Fastov avec de nombreuses pertes, les 1. SS, 2. SS et la 1. Pz bloquent l’avancée soviétique au sud.
Le 13 novembre, Von Manstein lance une contre-offensive avec le 48. Panzer Korps vers Jitomir et Brusilov dans le flanc sud de la pénétration soviétique…
Saurez vous, en tant que Soviétique, libérer Kiev pour l’anniversaire de la Révolution bolchévique et en tant qu’Allemand restaurer le front de la 4. Panzerarmee ?

—description from the publisher (French)

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Jyn Erso Commander Expansion
description:
The shadow of the Death Star looms across the galaxy, threatening any planet that doesn’t fall in line with the Empire. Only those brave—and perhaps foolish—enough to stand against the Empire can hope to save the galaxy from this technological terror. Raised by the extremist Saw Gerrera and on her own from a young age, Jyn Erso embodies both of these qualities.

Reckless and aggressive, Erso is nonetheless a brave leader who can contribute to your battle plan in many ways. Unafraid to wade into battle with her collapsible tonfa drawn, her quick thinking often gets her out of tricky situations. In fact, her Rebellious streak often comes in handy, letting her make a quick strike against an enemy unit or dive for cover before she can be targeted.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Rebel Pathfinders Unit Expansion
description:
Spies, saboteurs, and assassins, the Rebel Pathfinders are the most elite, and the most deadly, of the Alliance’s soldiers. These grizzled combat veterans need unwavering dedication to their cause in order to endure the horrors of their operations and missions. Those for volunteered for the Battle of Scarif in particular were willing to sacrifice everything for the dream of the Rebellion.

No strangers to operating behind enemy lines, a group of Rebel Pathfinders is the perfect special forces unit to take your enemy by surprise and keep them off-guard from the very beginning of a game. Since they’re usually already operating on the battlefield before an engagement starts, traditional deployment zones mean little to these troops. Instead, they can deploy anywhere on the battlefield beyond Range 3 of all enemy units. Once they’re in position, you’ll find the Rebel Pathfinders can quickly adapt to the changing tenor of a battle as they reconfigure their A-300 Blaster Rifles to be effective at both short and long range.

Countless stars, endless adventure! This expansion for Star Saga allows you to create your own mercenaries and mission, using the wide range of Mantic Miniatures available in the Warpath universe. Come up with a narrative, create your maps and campaigns and play though them with friends! The possibilities are endless!

—description from the publisher

As a clan leader, you will go on a mission with a small group to eliminate your archrival. You must complete this mission with six specialized warriors. Do you know how best to use your team's skills?

Players try to beat their opponents by using their dice in all sorts of combinations. This allows them to perform various attacks in order to damage the opponent's team. If a player succeeds in eliminating the clan leader or all of the opponent's attacking warriors, then that player has won the game.

Gather your friends and sit down to play LOOP: The Elevated Card Game. Ride the rails of the elevated trains in Chicago's famous Loop! Match the line colors and stations to make your way around town, but beware the everyday perils of rush hour crowds, forgotten farecards, and mysterious puddles as you get where you're going!

Gameplay is similar to UNO or Crazy Eights, but instead of matching numbers, suites, or colors, players match the L line or station. For example, if the top of the pile is a Brown Line card for the Washington/Wells station, you can play any other Brown Line, or one or more non-Brown Washington/Wells cards (as if you’re transferring lines in real life). The object of the game is to get rid of all of your cards first. The player who most recently used public transportation gets to deal.
—user summary

boardgame: City of Gears: Juggernaut Mini Expansion
description:
Was available as an add-on during the City of Gears Kickstarter campaign.

Includes its own rules, a unique gear, a special city tile and the juggernaut miniature! When choosing to play with the Juggernaut, you'll always add this tile to the middle of the map. Activating this tile causes the juggernaut to lumber into a neighboring tile, destroying an opponent's worker in the process.

A trick taking game where you become a cake shop and sell cakes to your customers. From beginners to experienced people can play.

Play Summary

Each turn, the Leader plays a card. He takes the "Customer" card with the corresponding cake and places it next to the just played cake card.
Then, the player to the left plays and then in clockwise order plays a card.

     Play a card with the same colour; if he can’t follow color, he places any color card in his correponding "Storage Area"
      "0" card of other other cake can be used as a wild card (i.e. can be used to follow the lead suit)
      If he plays a card with value 0-3, he can pull the cards from his "Storage Area" of that cake type and add all these to get a new total value
      Compare the sum of the just played card with previous played card(s)
      If the sum is same or smaller, he puts all cards into his own "Storage Area" of that cake type
      If the sum is larger, the previous card(s) is/are put into the owner’s "Storage Area" of that cake type. He gets the "Customer" card and place it beside his just played cake card(s).
      He chooses a card from the replenish row to refill his hand
      The Leader gets the final remaining card from the replenish row.


 
The player with card(s) still on the table is the winner. If he is the leader, he takes the cake card as his score and returns the "Customer" card to the supply. If a fellow player wins, he takes the cake card as his score. He also gets the "Customer" card and the "Start Player" card. He now scores 3x points for that cake.
 At the end of the game, each collected cake card scores 1 pt (3 pts 3 pts 3 pts with the corresponding "Customer" card). Who has the highest points wins.

Election! is a game about Australian Federal elections.

Players play as either government or opposition party through a number of days, represented by event cards, of an election campaign. Events give margin points to either party, and the player winning those points can play a strategy card for bonus points, which in turn can be countered by a strategy card played by their opponent. Once all event cards have been evaluated, the player with the most margin points wins the election, and thus the game.

boardgame: Fire! Fire! Scouts to the Rescue!
description:
The game entitled "Fire! Fire! Scouts to the Rescue" is a replica of a rare vintage 1920s board game of the same name.

Fire trucks and engines have always caught the imagination of children all over the world. Excitement mounts as the siren is heard coming closer and then the fire engine rushes by. This lovely board game is based on another era when the fire trucks and engines moved at a more sedate pace! Things have changed since the 1920s and 30s but this game is still great fun for all the family.

This is a Monopoly-style game with a "The Polar Express" theme. This collector's edition game was sold by the Masterpieces Inc. company.
—user summary

Relive the magic of The Polar Express adventure with this gameboard featuring the characters and places of the movie. Enjoy trivia, movie moments, and character quotes on the back of each property card. Each game contains a custom gameboard filled with gorgeous imagery and striking detail, 6 custom collectible pewter tokens, 28 property cards featuring interesting brand-themed trivia, 32 action cards, 1 pack of illustrated money, 44 custom designed plastic property markers, 2 dice, and illustrated rules. All Aboard!
—description from the publisher

In the Joker Cup, players will ride a jumping horses through the course with different obstacles. The dice indicates the speed of the horse but it is important to land on the right box to jump flawlessly.
But players may also use cards for helping the horses.
Who finds the shortest route to end is the winner.

UGO: Magic Garden is based on the highly praised UGO! In UGO: Magic Garden you are a wizard taking part in a yearly contest for becoming the best wizard. You're trying to plant exotic plants in your garden. There are easy, fertile patches of garden, but also patches that need the help of garden gnomes. You can also cheat by hiring goblins. But beware! At the end of the game, you'll be checked to see if you haven't cheated.

—description from the publisher

A card, turn-based strategy game without dice.

In Leida Heim each player take control of a Norse mythology hero. At the end of the game who earned more honor is proclaimed winner.
The game uses an action points system to make heroes move and perform actions.

—description from the publisher

Void Zone is a highly tactical pure strategy game set in the future where you pit your 2 robots against up to 3 other players in a constantly and dynamically shrinking arena. You win the game by out-manouvering your opponents and pushing them out of the playing area.

The game features a novel and highly thematic mechanic when the first player loses all their robots, the rate of arena shrink dramatically increases bringing a tense, engrossing, organic and rapid resolution to the game. The game is therefore designed to have a dramatic climax and if you are the first to go out, you will always want to see how it plays out.

Your robots have a Charge Chamber, which when filled can be used to perform upgraded actions to help give you the edge over your opponents.

Each turn you will perform actions with one robot, then perform actions with your other robot on your next turn.

On your turn, you will perform up to 3 different actions or the same action twice:
- Run. Move your robot round the arena. Each space on the board can only be travelled over twice so how you move is criticial to your success or demise.
- Jolt. Knock opponent robots (or even your own) one space to gain a tactical and positional advantage!
- Place Force Shields. Inpenetrable shields that you can place adjacent to your robot to use for area control or to help push opponents out
- Drain / Recharge Arena. Use this to take some power from the arena and power up your charge chamber, putting you at more risk or replenish the arena to give you some breathing space
- Jump. As the game progresses you will need this to help get you out of a corner or to cause spectacular KO's
- Upgrade Charge Chamber Capacity. You want to get more powerful actions? Of course you do!

If you find yourself in a tight spot and see no way out then don't worry! You can use your Prime token to give you some breathing space or to open a path to victory.

There are multiple variable player powers which provides great replayability and for more variants, there are rules for House Robots who add to the mayhem!

—description from the designer

A party game for those who feel the need to say something completely inappropriate. Incorrect politically, hilarious and eccentric.

To start the game, each player draws eight white cards. One player becomes a Reader. He randomly draws a black card and reads the question or fill-in-the-blanks phrase out loud. Each player chooses an answer from white cards kept in hand and passes it face down to the Reader. The Reader shuffles received cards and successively reads the answers and arranges them on the table. It's best to read the answers together with the question matching the syntax where it's needed. Some questions require two white cards. In this case, the Reader does not shuffle them and reads in the order arranged by players. When all responses have been read, each player points the finger at the funniest answer. The player whose answer got the most votes wins the round and gets the black card as a reward. When it's a draw, each of the drawers wins and gets a black card. Before next round everyone draws cards up to eight, the next player becomes a Reader. The game lasts until no cards, time or participants left. The player who earned the most black cards wins.

—user summary

In this game, 3-6 players are Greek Gods that secretly support in a friendly or enemy way the geek hero Ulysses, coming back from the war of troy.

To come back to Itacha, Ulysses will face 7 quests like the encounter with Polyphemos, the journey in the hades, the resistance of the mermaides’ song and the meeting with Circe, the witch. In each of these encounters, each God can Change Ulysses’ main abilities (strenght, guile, oratory, luck and charisma) to make the journey easier or… much harder!

Play your abilities cards in the right moment, use special powers and try to understand who is your friend and who is your enemy as soon as possible to win this game beatiful illustrated by the italian artist “Idras”.

Odyssey is the second game of the Greek series, after Mythomakya designed by Luca Feliciani.

Murder mystery party game for 8-12 players that you host at home. Each player dresses up as a character, one is the murderer and the rest must figure out who that is. To solve the mystery there are clues, police report, crime scene photos, and a map. You go over these materials then question all the players about what their characters did and saw to finally solve who done it. The game lasts a times 2.5 hours.

Remember the excitement and glamour of your Senior Prom? Well now you can relive it, 1980s style. McKinley High School is having their Senior Prom, and like your typical prom there will be punch, snacks, a student rock band, and the coronation of the prom king and queen.
This could be any American population, with the in-crowd, jocks, geeks, dopers, losers, and even the well liked, super cool drug dealer. Don’t you remember how fun it all was?
On this particular prom night though there is more going on than your standard spiked punch and necking in a dark corner. Real murder and mayhem is brewing. By the end of the night you will be shocked and astonished and will have lost a considerable amount of respect for your fellow students and the school faculty (who would have thought!).
So throw on a boutonniere, squeeze into that tiny gown, fluff your hair up big and get ready to boogey on down with some punch, canapes, cookies, and some drop dead entertainment.

—description from the publisher

You’ve been hired by Walking Doggos, a subsidiary of Good Boyes, Inc.

Your job? Walking the goodest doggos every day for minimum wage while their owners are at work.

It doesn’t matter how many dogs you walk, or how hard you work, you get paid the same no matter what.

So, what does a clever slacker like you do? As little as possible without getting fired.

Walking Doggos is a trick-taking game, where the goal is to take fewer tricks than your opponents while still at least winning one trick. There are six suits of cards: Great Danes, Labradors, Shiba Inus, Beagles, Corgis, and Pugs ranked by size of the breed. Each suit has a card numbered 1 through 8 representing the number of that breed that need to be walked.

Players each have a hand of 5 cards held face out, so that only other players can see what cards are being held. Players take turns giving clues to other players as to what cards are in their hand. Once every player has 3 clues, players take turns betting how many tricks they are going to take. After all players have made their bets, players take turns playing a card face up into a pack to be walked. The player who played the highest ranked doggo wins the pack. Players get points based on the number of packs they take compared to what they bet. The winner is the player who did the least amount of work necessary without being fired.

-description from designer

description:
CAB! is an educational card game based on the parts of speech. It involves turn-taking, incidental learning, peer teaching, spelling and grouping. 
The game has the potential to improve vocabulary and literacy skills while engaging students in a fun experience.

OBJECTIVE
Be the first person to get rid of all your cards
  
SETUP
Each person draws a card. The player who’s card has the greatest number of letters is the dealer. 
The dealer shuffles the deck and gives each player 7 cards.

Then places the remainder of the deck face down to create a ‘pick up’ pile. 
The dealer takes the top card off the ‘pick up’ pile and puts it face up, to begin the ‘put down’ pile.

START PLAYING
The person to the left of the dealer begins the game.

When it’s your turn you must put down a card matching the top card on the ‘put down’ pile, either being the same type of word (adjective, noun, verb or conjunction) or having a matching punctuation sign
(!   ,   ?   ‘   :   “”  . ). 
You can also put down any ‘action card’ (see action card).
 
EXAMPLE: If the top card on the “put down’ pile is the word ‘happy’ with a : on it, the next person must put down either another adjective card, a card with a : on it or a action card (see Action Cards).
 
If you don’t have a card that matches the card on top of the ‘put down’  pile you must pick up a card from the top of the ‘pick up’ pile.  This is the end of your turn.

ACTION CARDS
The following action cards can be placed on the ‘put down’ pile at any point during your turn, no matter what the top card on the pile is. When an action card is played the word type is automatically changed to the same as the word on the card being played.

Pick Up 2  – When this card is played the next player must pick up two cards and cannot put down any cards. If they have a ‘pick up’ card as well they can then play that card, making the next person pick up four etc.
Miss A Go  – When this card is played the next person misses their turn. 
Go Back  – When this card is played the direction of play is reversed. 
Spell It – When this card is played the player putting the card down picks up a card from the ’pick up’ pile and asks the player, who's turn it is next, to spell the word. If the word is spelt correctly, the card is placed on the ‘put down’ pile and the speller takes his turn. If the word is spelt incorrectly, the card is given to the speller and they miss their turn. The next player in the sequence then starts their turn.

WINNING
The first person to get rid of all their cards is the winner!

-description from designer

boardgame: The Epic Story of Blank & Blank
description:
The Epic Story of Blank & Blank is a card game revolving around storytelling. With just one image card and one text card, players tell their epic tale of either world domination in the role of the monster, or salvation in the role of the hero. The basic version is accessible for nearly everyone and can be explained in two minutes.


     Play as a monster or hero
     Create your own universe with the cards dealt
     See what friends can come up with in two minutes
     Unleash the creativity in a room with stories you won't expect


—description from the designer

A hex-and-counter game covering this most decisive Russian Front battle, 30Sep41-31Jan42 in 8 semi-monthly turns at 20 miles - 32 kilometers - per hex and basically corps to army scale.  Map scale is same as for SPI's now classic War in Europe.  German operational intelligence limited by Russian maskirova/deception pieces.

4 especially strong panzer divisions are broken out for deep penetration and exchange small change.

Simple airpower units and rules, including Russian paradrops ... if both korps aren't already eaten up in the ground battle.

Unpredictable, press-your-luck combat system for risk-takers, along with extra German offensive phases - one at the start and another later ... which also makes for good solitaire play.

And my famous/notorious Siberian Reserve Gamble.  :-)

Building on the success of Ophidian 2360, this release features a completely new/updated rulebook, expanded card pool for multiplayer support, durable storage box, dice, tokens, and more!  Arenaverse: Homeworlds is a fully upgraded and self-contained experience!

Take your team of gladiators and crush your opponents!

Arenaverse: Homeworlds features unique momentum-based mechanics, allowing players to chain together actions to form combinations, as well as gaining the favor of the crowd.  But be careful, your opponent can also 'cash in' some of their popularity with the crowd to break up your combos!  With multiple paths to victory and main characters that can level up throughout the game, gaining new abilities, every game is a story of action and mayhem!

—description from the publisher

Master Smuggle is a single card worth 10 gold. It reads: 'Set this card aside before the game. At the end of game, give this card to the merchant who delivered the second most contraband.

The promo appeared in 2017 and was later made part of the 2018 promo pack.

Planetex is a game for 2 to 4 players from 60 to 90 minutes long. The players embody space carriers. Playing their action cards, they will have to travel the solar system collecting resources and delivering them to the planets that require them fulfilling missions and improving their ship.

Planetex es un juego para 2 a 4 jugadores de 60 a 90 minutos de duración. Los jugadores encarnan a transportistas espaciales. Jugando sus cartas de acción deberán recorrer el sistema solar recogiendo recursos y entregándolos en los planetas que los requieran cumpliendo misiones y mejorando su nave.

boardgame: Penguin Brawl: Heroes of Pentarctica – Nuclear Winter Expansion
description:
Nuclear Winter is the 2nd micro-expansion for Penguin Brawl and adds 30 new cards comprising of six penguins and their associated gear, spells and instants.

Nuclear Winter reinvents some of the more loved characters from the Penguin Brawls original cast in an apocalyptic wasteland, where they must fight tooth and nail simply to survive. This expansion takes close note of Curses and adds six new and interesting Curse cards to the game. Reinvented characters include; The Frozen Phoenix, Van Helking, Long John Shivers, The Mad Flipper, Baron Penedi and Emperor Tesla. The new versions of these characters can be used alongside their counterparts from the original game to increase combo chances and face-off opportunities.

A natural disaster (or three!) has ravaged your town. People need food, water, and supplies, and time is running out. You need money now and lots of it. Can you navigate the twists and turns of utter destruction to save the lives of everyone in town before nature strikes again?
Master of Disaster is an educational board game that teaches about natural disasters and what to do if one hits in a fun and interactive way. Each team, or agency, has to protect the US from disasters, purchasing insurance-like protection based on risk and cost estimates. They earn money answering questions on the disasters. Each agency hopes to have to most money left at the end.
