Monkey (2x): Replaces one Powder Monkey from each player‘s starting deck. The Monkey creates a loop in the pirate ranks. It fits between CPT and 1. Thus a straight CPT-M-1 is now possible. Additionally, the suit of the Monkey card is wild. You can decide the suit when assembling a raiding party.

Present (1x): Take the top treasure from the treasure discard pile. Sell or keep.

Paper Pinball is a dice game that tries to capture the feeling of playing pinball. All you will need is a pencil, three dice, and about five minutes.

Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multi-ball.
family: Series: Paper Pinball (Metal Snail Idea Workshop)

description:
Don’t let the dim light of the full moon mislead you. Tonight Dark City is shadow territory. Monsters prowl the streets, craving for blood, and they’ll do anything to keep their hunting grounds. Humankind has its guardians, however few and far between, and they fight for their lives with matching ferocity. Who will survive another showdown on the gloomy streets of Dark City?

Dark City is a party game in which players choose iconic characters from the cast of a horror movie – vampires, werewolves, zombies, hunters, priests – then face their opponents in a fierce battle of wits, inference and finger-pointing.

Every round, the Acting Player randomly discards three cards from the deck. Then, one by one, players draft a monster or guardian at their choice from among the remaining cards. Players attack their chosen target by aiming their monster’s or guardian’s signature gesture at it. Then players have a chance to flee the battle, trying to prevent their opponents from scoring. Attacks are resolved in initiative order. Successful attacks “kill” their targets and allow players to achieve their monster’s or guardian’s goals. Players whose monsters or guardians have “survived” the encounter and achieved their goals score experience points (XP).The game ends when one or more players gather enough XP to meet at least one of the four victory conditions.

—description from the publisher

Contract will be received for 5 days (rounds) from Monday to Friday. It is a game in which players earn money to construct a port facility, process contracts of contracts, and win he highest victory points.


     This game is available for 2 to 4 players.
     Players play for 5 rounds from Monday to Friday.
     Each round consumes an optimal amount of time, depending on you situation, for an average of eight hours. Apart from that, 4 hours each round can be used at an additional cost. This is 8 hours a day you have to work overtime and pay for it.
     You can become a winner of the game by optimizing your time and taking advantage of it.

Card game combining memory and risk taking with a cooperative version.

The cards "the wolf arrives" are placed face up, each player receives a house card and we place all other cards face down on the table. Each player in turn returns as many cards as he wishes in order to reveal and recover the animals from his house. But he must stop and win nothing if he returns a card he has already revealed or if he returns a wolf card. In the latter case he returns a card "the wolf arrives", unless he returned a card clover. When a player has collected the 4 animals from his house, he puts the cards aside and draws a new house. The game is over when all the cards "the wolf arrives" are returned or when all the animals are safe. This is the player with the most cards "animal" who wins. In the cooperative version the players play together against the wolf!

—user summary

Quantum Wordcraft is a crossword style game for all ages. Each player draws 7 cards and then rolls two dice for bonus categories or an extra card. Cards are worth 1-4 points depending on rarity.

It has similarities to Scrabble, but without the need for a bulky game board. No need for a board makes it very portable. You can also play words over other words.

—description from the publisher

Westbrooke: Out Last is a zombies vs. humans game that pits you against time and each other. Work with your team of fellow survivors to escape the zombie ridden city of Westbrooke before it gets destroyed by a nuclear bomb.

As humans you must move from tile to tile locating objectives, fighting off zombies, and collecting items and weapons. Use the custom dice to fight off zombies. Once you and your teammates have collected the objectives make you must make your way to the exit before the nuke gets dropped.

The opposing zombie team places zombies of all sorts in the path of the humans to try and stop them from reaching the exit. If they are going down they are taking the humans with them.

The Deluxe Edition contains two additional characters — the Priest and the Scout — as well as 18 extra Phase cards (items, weapons, and strategy cards).

Don't look at TV shows anymore, create them!

You are TV show writers and together, you'll create the new successful TV series.
To be crowned best scriptwriter, you'll have to know every details by heart.
Tell your TV plot by playing location, character and event cards and remember a maximum of details to win the game!

boardgame: Fog of Love: New Occupation Promo Cards
description:
12 new occupation cards:
-Car salesperson
-Aid worker
-Prison Guard
-Nanny
-Construction worker
-Relationship counsellor
-Online seller
-Singer
-Tax collector
-Photographer
-Surfer
-Cleaner

Contained in Tabletop Gaming Magazine January 2019 issue.

Simple Roll and Move game featuring characters from the movie Cars 3. Players use a spinner to move along a racetrack with obstacles like flat tires, oil slicks or suddenly running out of fuel.

—user summary

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.  All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset. 
Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag.  Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

This set contains the Me-163, the only rocket-powered aircraft to see combat.  Scenarios include:
Bat Out Of Hell:  Komets bounce a flight of P-38 Lightnings
Point Defense:  Me-163 Komets assisted Fw-190D fighters attacking a formation of B-17G bombers and their P-51D escorts.
Red Komet:  Returning from a mission with no fuel, a lone Me-163 has to glide past marauding P-51D fighters to return to base.

A fast-paced, action, skirmish wargame in 32mm scale, set in a dystopian science-fantasy universe. Starcide reaches between the realms of sci-fi and fantasy with a dazzling variety of factions to select from including:

Legion of the Black Sun (blood-thirsted vampires and Soul-rending necromancers), Empire of Man (Unified Noble Houses of mankind) , Na'vo (cold-blooded reptiles), Prometheans (a human-like Psionic race), and the Locust (deadly insectoids with biotechnological space travel).

—description from the publisher

A fun party game about everything you thought that you and your friends thinks in the same way.

One of the players reads the question from the card. Team members have 30 seconds to try to guess what the partners will answer and write the same answer separately.

The group that received the most correct answers can move the button forward on the board.

The winner is the team that reaches 30 points before the others.

Hemispheres pits 2 halves of the same brain against each other. Each half is trying to control the entire brain. To do this, each player has the same 8 cards as their opponent.  Each player is trying to out think their opponent to swing the personality meter all the way to their side of the brain and take total control.

Turns are played simultaneously by choosing the card to play each round and then revealing them together (along with each cards special Trait chosen that turn).  After applying the Traits, the final Power values are compared to affect the Node track (score).  Cards have a cool off period before returning to the hand.  Choosing the right Power value and right Trait becomes a mind game against your opponent.  This game is best played against the same opponent repeatedly to get into each others's brains.

This game was originally designed for the 2016 2-Player PnP Contest.

—description from the designer

User summary
ジグザ (Jiguza) (ZIXZA) is a two-player game based on a 1980's cyberpunk novel where hackers and guards fight in the cyber space.

In a diagonal square grid, each player places their three dice on the spaces nearest to them.

During your turn, you take one of three actions below.
ROLL: Move one of your dice rotating vertically over to an adjacent space.
TURN: Rotate one of your dice 90 degrees horizontally, 
ATTACK: Remove one of opponent's dice if your die adjacent to it has greater number.

You win the game when one of three conditions is met.
HOLD: Occupy all three squares at center of the grid with your dice.
GET: Remove two of opponent's dice.
ARRIVE: Occupy a square farthest from you with your die.

Repeatedly enjoyable and thought-provoking game system with less luck involved. 
You can play this game anywhere with postcard-sized game board.

Two sets of three dice of the same color required to play this game.

Publisher's summary
ZIXZA（ジグザ）は80年代サイバーパンク小説をモチーフにした、 
電脳世界でハッカーとガードに分かれプレイする2人用対戦ゲーム！
『移動（ROLL）』『回転（TURN）』『攻撃（ATTACK）』の3つの方法で、ダイスを操作、 
『占拠（HOLD）』『奪取（GET）』『到達（ARRIVE）』の3つの方法で勝利するシンプルなルール。
運要素の少ないシステムと、繰り返し楽しめる思考性。
ポストカードサイズのコンパクトなデザインなので 
遊ぶ場所を選びません！！

注意：遊ぶ際に2色3つずつのダイスをご用意ください。

Fantasy themed game for 2-5 players where the board changes every time you play. Play cards from your hand against defending dice rolls in order to take control of land and net victory points for your faction. Brave wild encounters to attain powerful enhancements or gain helpful advantages without risk. Through the 10 rounds collect the most victory points in order to become the leading force on the board at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The Golden Girls Trivial Pursuit bite-size edition contains 600 questions based on all seven seasons of the Emmy Award-winning TV series.

Create magical mischief with Unicorn Surprise! In this whimsical game, players are transformed into pixies on the hunt for colorful unicorn gems. The only problem – unicorns drop their Gems but once a year! Race against other cheeky pixie players to collect the most gems before the smiling sun sets. Unicorn Surprise combines board game suspense with a silly unicorn figure that pixie players can interact with. Will she drop a colored gem or a clear one signifying the end of the day? Find out when you play Unicorn Surprise!

—description from the publisher

A quick two player pattern matching game using a double-sided connect-four-esque board. Each turn, players roll a die to determine what color token they can add to the board, and then try to match one of the shown 3-token patterns. The board and pattern cards are circular, and each of the tokens are double sided with two colors, creating pleasing aesthetics.

boardgame: Ancient Terrible Things: Unnatural Mystic Promo Card
description:
Kickstarter exclusive limited edition adventure promo card (from the Ancient Terrible Things: AwakeTen Kickstarter).

An additional adventure card that can be added to the deck of adventure cards in the base game.

Catch Me! is a simple card game for 2 to 6 players. Each player receives a stack of face down cards displaying patterns in different colours. In the middle of the table there's a wooden stick up for grabs. Every round, a player turns over one of their face down cards. If two players have cards of the same pattern in front of them, both players compete to "catch" the stick first. The loser of this duel receives the winner's active cards to add to their stack. Whoever gets rid of all their cards first wins the game!

Duel cards are included as a variant.

—user summary

Have you ever planted a forest? If not, it's time!

Here you can hear birds singing, Help me through the trees!

In this game, we invite you to plant the forest! Your profit will be to grow five trees. Each of them consists of four parts. You roll two dice, and in the factory you choose more elements. The person who is the first to grow five trees wins.

The box comes with a seed! They are tiny, but do not be fooled by appearances. Giant trees will grow out of them!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Mount Gundabad
description:
They marched and gathered by hill and valley, going ever by tunnel or under dark, until around and beneath the great mountain Gundabad of the North, where was their capital, a vast host was assembled ready to sweep down in time of storm unawares.
   –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Hobbit

Mount Gundabad is the fifth Adventure Pack in the Ered Mithrin cycle.

The time has come. All your adventures across Wilderland have pointed you in one direction: toward Mount Gundabad and a confrontation with the terrible Dragon that roosts within. While few would openly rush into a battle with such a beast, one thing remains certain: Dagnir poses a threat to the whole of Wilderland and you must take on whatever risks are necessary to keep it safe.

Take the next step in your journey with Mount Gundabad! With new player cards to diversify your decks and an entirely new adventure to challenge your skills, you have everything you need to turn your footsteps toward Mount Gundabad.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
A solitaire sports replay game which enables the player to replay Cycling Grand Tours stage by stage.
Chart and dice system, which requires a race data book for each tour. Each data book contains the ratings of each cyclist in the particular tour.
The race unfolds stage by stage. It allows for time gaps, mountain points and finish line sprint points to be totalled.
Flat stages hilly stages, mountain stages and time trials are all included.

酒魅人 is a game about making SAKE (Japanese Rice Wine.) 
The player becomes the owner of SAKE brewery and decides which process to focus on SAKE made by himself.
All the attention boards dealt to players are the same. Every round player should bidding for the sequence to get the materiel. For that reason, it is important what kind of criteria you will score on sake made by yourself.

Because the evaluation point of sake is not equal to the victory point, you can scoop up your feet if you are too busy drinking!
No knowledge of sake is necessary, bow hits competing with each other!
publishers: Super Number One Games (スーパー ナンバーワン ゲームズ)

description: This release is part of a 3-item set released in 2.5 inch round metal tins by University games. This game includes 30 second mysteries of "Solve the Mystery", "Solve the Mystery Gram", and "Solve the Brainteaser". Each includes 64 cards.

Is tonight a night where you want insane family fun, or do you feel ruthless, and want to show your competitive side?

Zombies The Cure is a board game where you and your family will have just pure crazy family fun.

Zombies The Cure board game turns into a crazy fun card game. Take the game deck of cards combined with all the expansion packs then becomes a very competitive stand-alone card game for you and your friends to play.

So... go ahead and have some pure crazy family fun tonight.

A Government experiment has gone badly wrong, turning human and creature into the not-so-dead Zombies, monsters, freaks, and some ugly but cute little critters! Zombies are leading to the collapse of civilization, the not-so-dead, or not-so-living is taking over the living, unless it is dealt with quickly all hope will be lost. A cure exists, all you must do is pick it up and carry it to the lab, but be careful trust no one, not even your friends they will kill you, to be the one who takes the cure to the lab.

Zombies The Cure board game requires a mixture of luck and strategy, making it a fun, unpredictable game for the whole family. This kid-friendly, super-competitive, killer-fun game is Just Pure Crazy Family Fun.

The material can also be used to play 9RUN the card game, which can be played one single round or can be played with multiple rounds to see who has the highest stockpile points at the end. Just Pure Crazy Family Fun.

The object is to get your colored pawn to the lab with the cure.

Players can build up an army of cards that allow them to infect you, or sending you to the graveyard where you are forced you to escape or die.

A player may also steal your cure, even killing you and sending you back to start forcing you to begin all over again!

In this game of twist and turns, you'll need lots of skill and a little luck to maneuver your way of picking up the cure and making your way to the lab.

—description from the publisher

Trivial Pursuit: Rick and Morty allows fans of the hit television series to test their knowledge from 600 questions relating to Frienemies, Technology & Science, Locations, Rick and the Family, History & Events and a Wubalubadubdub Wild Card category.

Owning a growing restaurant is hard work. An aspiring restaurant entrepreneur must make wise decisions to manage day-to-day operations and keep loyal customers happy, while always innovating to draw new customers through the door. But beware, competition is fierce as other restaurateurs are also looking to build their own reputations and secure their own loyal customers.

In Dining Dynasties, each player must work to draw in business, find loyal customers and create exciting new dishes! Players manage all aspects of their restaurant in a fast paced competition with their rivals to offer the finest cuisine and carve out a share of the ever changing customer base. To do this, players must build and maintain a hand of cards to create a unique business plan of options. Players may focus on marketing to advertise for new customers and seek out unique sales opportunities, improve the mood of the restaurant by building a professional staff and improving restaurant décor, or a combination of all. Yet another option is to invest in the menu and seek to claim the title of finest cuisine among the competitors! A unique meta-game within each game round has players strategically playing menu cards to vie for the title of finest cuisine, which may provide an immediate valuable customer bonus.

Dining Dynasties also allows players to increase the restaurant size. Do you want a small gourmet bistro or a huge dining establishment? The options are yours to choose, with each new room tile providing new worker placement opportunities to further your restaurant goals. But be cautious, expanding at the wrong time can take a toll on your available cash.

Over the course of nine rounds, players strive to build the most valuable restaurant by earning reputation, increasing sales and building infrastructure. The player who chooses the most effective business plan, and best mitigates the ups and downs of the market, will emerge the winner.

-description from designer

IAM is an UNO-style card game with a party game overlay.

Each player starts with 4-7 cards in hand based on the player count. On a turn, play a card from your hand that matches either the number or color of the top card on the discard pile, then ask a question of a fellow player within a number of seats to you equal to the number on the card. (If you play a 2, for example, you must ask either your neighbor or the player next to them, going either clockwise or counter-clockwise depending on the direction of play.)

The topic of your question must be related to the card you just played, with blue cards relating to daily life, green society and community, red love and friendship, and yellow private matters. If the chosen player cannot answer or chooses not to, they must draw two cards, then the next player plays. If they answer the question, then they get to play a card. The game includes special cards, so the player questioned might be able to bounce the question back to the original questioner, pass it on to the next player, or require more people to answer as well.

If you have no matching cards on your turn, you draw a card and your turn ends. Alternatively, you might play a special card that reverses turn order or changes the color of cards that can be played.

Whoever plays all of their cards first wins!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
User summary
トポロメモリー (TOPOLOMEMORY) is a Japanese card game based on the traditional matching game Karuta.

Topolomemory is a concentration game in which you have to match shapes using the principles of  Topology. In it's basic form, topology shows us that a doughnut is the same as a coffee mug.

Lay out the tiles with various images on them and whoever most quickly identifies pairs that match topologically gets to collect the pair. The player with the most pairs wins the game. The game game be played by more than five players, especially in teams.

Publisher's summary
トポロジーを利用して行う、図形の神経衰弱ゲームです。
場に図形が描かれたカードを並べていき、トポロジー的に同じ形のペアを探し、早いもの勝ちで取り合います。
最も多くのペアを集めたプレイヤーが勝利です。
publishers: Banso Inc. (株式会社バンソウ)

description:
This game is based in London in 1890, and the player is setting up a department store.  The store consists of a number of departments, which each need to be filled with stock.  Customers come to the store with a list of items that need to be bought.  If all items are sold by a single player's department store, then that player wins that customer.  If the customer still has items remaining to be bought, then they move onto the next player's department.

This is mainly a card-based game, where customers, items to be bought, and departments are represented by cards, and stock is represented by small wooden cubes.  On a player's turn, they can choose a customer from 3 available customers, who each have a number of items that they want to buy, the number of which are decided by the roll of a dice.  Only the number of items to buy is visible to the player, not the actual items themselves, so it's only when the customer is chosen that the items are revealed and the player knows what can be sold.  Players receive money for items sold, which can then be used to buy new stock or new departments at the end of their turn.

The game relies on players choosing the right departments to set up, depending on what other players have already chosen, and deciding which departments should get restocked and at what time, especially when money is limited.  A player wins by being the player with the highest number of customers that had all items sold during one turn.  There are bonus points available for having the majority of customers in each family of customer.

Game contains 70 cards, 50 wooden cubes, 1 dice, 45 £1/£2/£5/£10 notes and a rule book.

-description from designer
Build your way through the board in QBO2 and take care to wipe your tracks. Special tiles support you to play offensively and defensively.

This 88-page softcover book has rules for a whole new way of playing Warhammer Fantasy Battle (5th Edition) focused on sieging a castle. New rules for things like siege engines and dropping items, say boiling oil; a new flow for play; and a system for buying castle defenses within the scope of building Fantasy armies are all presented.

boardgame: The Cauldron: Adrift (fan expansion for Sentinels of the Multiverse)
description:
The Adrift set for the unofficial fan expansion The Cauldron, intended for use with the Sentinels of the Multiverse card game by Greater Than Games, includes new heroes, villains, and environments.

The following decks are included in this set:

Heroes:

     Drift
     Gargoyle
     Gyrosaur
     Pyre
     Terminus


Villains:

     Dynamo
     The Infernal Choir
     The Mistress of Fate
     Mythos
     Screamachine


Environments:

     Catchwater Harbor 1929
     The Chasm of a Thousand Nights
     Nightlore Citadel
     Vault 5
     Windmill City

boardgame: The Cauldron: Promo Pack #3 (fan expansion for Sentinels of the Multiverse)
description:
Promo Pack #3 from the unofficial fan expansion The Cauldron, intended for use with the Sentinels of the Multiverse card game by Greater Than Games, contains alternate character cards for Cauldron hero and villain decks.

These decks — which come from The Cauldron: Base Set, The Cauldron: Experimental, The Cauldron: Stormfall, and The Cauldron: Adrift — are available separately and required to play.

The following cards are included in this promo pack:


     Wasteland Ronin Cricket
     Swarming Protocol Cypher
     2199 Doc Havoc
     1929/2199 Dual Drift
     Through the Breach Drift
     2199 Echelon
     2199 Gargoyle
     Wasteland Ronin Gargoyle
     Renegade Gyrosaur
     Speed Demon Gyrosaur
     Wasteland Ronin Impact
     2199 Lady of the Wood
     The Unstable Pyre
     Wasteland Ronin Pyre
     Wasteland Ronin Stranger
     2199 Terminus
     Ministry of Strategic Science Terminus
     2199 Titan
     2E Vanish

A game of convention goals, but mostly horseplay.

All of the fun (and sometimes frustration) of attending a convention in one convenient game!

Collect swag, signatures, art or attend panels and other events to accumulate the most "fun" or "points" and have the best convention ever!

As with most conventions, however, sometimes things don't go quite as planned as your time quickly slips away.

Play against your friends and inconvenience them from obtaining their goals while attempting to achieve your own!

—description from the publisher

In Doomsday, players take on the roles of the last inhabitants of the island kingdom of Atlantis.  Earthquakes are ravaging the island and large chunks of it keep falling into the sea!  You have to keep moving each round, trying to find the safest space on the island.  Those who choose unwisely will fall into the sea and be lost forever!

All is not lost, however.  Boreas, the wind god, has taken pity on the doomed Atlanteans and has pledged to save the most worthy of you.  Whoever can survive to the end will be swept up by Boreas and carried to safety.  Do you have what it takes to outsmart your opponents and live to tell the tale?

Doomsday is a card game that integrates with a mobile app to create a fun, light-hearted party game. It is a social-deduction game of bluffing, persuasion, back stabbing and reading your opponents.  Each round, players simultaneously select a location card from their hand to travel to.  If too many people travel to the same location, the mobile app will eliminate some of them.

Location cards have special abilities that may modify the usual flow of the game, but play will continue until there are two players left who face off in a showdown.  Depending on their choices, there may be one winner, two winners, or they may both lose!  Games are quick and designed to be played multiple times in succession.

—description from the publisher

Turn by turn in NU2, you choose tiles to build a path between your pawns: one path forward and one path back. Choose wisely, or you might be help another player to win the game.

—description from the publisher

Be the first to find the opposite cookie to win the round! Cinnamon or chocolate? Round or square? Solid or hollow? Plain or textured? Wavy or straight?

Who will spot the right goodie first to win Box of Cookies?

Shadows of the Academy is a cooperative card game designed for students in grades 4-7. Created as part of Alberta Health's tobacco prevention curriculum, the game has players take the roles of students in a mysterious academy, trying to defeat dastardly villains while keeping their friends safe. The students all have two unique ability cards that may be played once during the game.

Players win when they defeat 5 villains, but they lose if any player is unable to draw at the beginning of their turn, or if the deck of cards is depleted before all 5 villains are defeated.

Players may play cards from their hand to heal themselves or their peers, or to weaken villains. Harm cards lower hand size, so keeping an eye on each other is an integral part of the gameplay, as cards drawn at the end of a round could be helpful, they could be harmful, or they could be another villain to fight!

-description from designer

A version of the classic board game based on the tragically hilarious animated Netflix series. Players take on the role of talent agents pining to sign the best clients by buying, selling, and trading Production Companies and Studios in Hollywoo!
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
This is the second edition of very popular Warhammer Fantasy Battles wargame, released shortly after the first edition.

Warhammer Fantasy Battles is a tabletop game for two or more players where you command several armies from a huge range that goes from Humans, Dwarves, Elves, Undead, Orcs, Lizardmen, and other fantasy races. This starter set provides you with all the rules you need to play, a core for two armies, and guides you through collecting and painting your own army of Citadel miniatures.

This edition consists of three books: Combat, Magic, and Battle Bestiary.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Take command of your very own pirate ship, and blast off to the rings and moons of Saturn! The high seas are no match for the adventures that await you and your crew as you race against rivals, and battle for resources and treasures. You'll need split-second planning to outmaneuver and outwit the other captains if you want to claim this section of space as your own!

Folding Space is a highly approachable game for 3 to 6 players, ages 10 and up, that takes approximately 30-45 minutes to play.  The game features an innovative infinity board for all players to give orders to their crew simultaneously, and those players will pillage resources, acquire treasure maps, and battle their space pirate rivals on a modular tile map.

The object is to not let any raptors fall down the enclosure during your turn. Create a web of colored sticks across the tree and place the raptors inside the enclosure. Then roll the die and take turns removing one stick at a time—whichever color stick the die indicates. Be careful because one false move will cause raptors to fall and then you have to add them to your pile! The player to drop the fewest raptors wins!

However, if a player causes Blue to fall, that player wins the game even if he has more raptors than other players.

Game comes with 1 bright yellow, translucent plastic palm tree so you can see the raptors wriggle as you try to catch their tails on plastic sticks. Also included are 30 raptors, 30 multiple colored sticks, and a special die.

boardgame: ダイスガールズ Track２ (Dice Girls Track 2)
description:
ダイスガールズ　Track２ (Dice Girls Track 2)

ダイスガールズ (Dice Girls) is a doujin game themed on pop singer groups from Japanese video games named "The Idolmaster" and "The Idolmaster Cinderella Girls".

This stand-alone expansion set of Dice Girls contains 18 additional Idol cards. The game can be played with any number of players so long as each player is dealt 6 cards, but game plays with up to 6 players is recommended.

日本のビデオゲーム「アイドルマスター」、「シンデレラガールズ」をモチーフにした同人ゲームです。
前作「ダイスガールズ」の独立型エキスパンションセットです。
18人のアイドルが追加されます。
１プレイヤーに６枚のカードがあれば、何人でも遊べますが
最大でも６人程度をお勧めします。

boardgame: Emojinks: A Fast-Paced Emoji Card Game
description: This is a fast paced game in which each player has a card with a number of emoji images. Each pair of cards have exactly one matching image. The goal of the game is to find the matching image faster than your opponents. There are two sides to each card: an easy side and a more advanced side.

boardgame: Warhammer: The Game of Fantasy Battles (6th Edition Boxed Set)
description: The boxed set for the sixth edition of Warhammer contained the rulebook and two small forces, Orcs vs. Empire.

Ravening Hordes is a complete set of 15 army lists for the 6th edition of Warhammer: The Game of Fantasy Battles, that allows to use existing armies (from the 4th, and 5th edition) in the new version of the game.

Armies in the 32-page booklet: Orcs & Goblins, Empire, Daemon Hosts, Chaos Warriors, Beastmen, Skaven, Tomb Kings of Khemri, Vampire Counts, Dwarfs, Lizardmen, High Elves, Wood Elves, Dark Elves, Bretonnians, Chaos Dwarfs.

Warhammer: Annual 2002 is an expansion to the 6th edition of Warhammer: The Game of Fantasy Battles.

This 144-page softcover book is a compilation of the best Warhammer articles from the pages of White Dwarf Magazine and the Games Workshop web site, plus much revised and previously unpublished material.

Warhammer: Chronicles 2004 is an expansion to the 6th edition of Warhammer: The Game of Fantasy Battles.

This 128-page softcover book is a compilation Warhammer articles, combined with revised or previously unpublished material.

Riddle Cube is a quick, family game where up to 4 players pull a malleable cube with elasticated string components to race to see who can construct the desired shape in a minute. Points are scored for the quickest player to construct the shape. Pretty simple game.

boardgame: Crisis: The Economist International Money Game
description:
Crisis is a board game that was included with a 1990's issue of The Economist magazine.

The game focuses on the international bullion and currency markets.

Each player is dealt a mixed hand of currencies and gold, and attempts - through bluffing and card play - to establish monopolies in one or more holdings.

The game is a mixture of roll and move combined with card play, management and counting.

The game provides rules and board, but the players must provide cards and a die.

There is no designer credited.

The Wheel of Fortune card stack is played on a 4 sun shards spot on the game board.

Immediate effect: receive divine blessings once except in this one case: before rolling all the dice, pick and declare a roll outcome for each dice among all the likely outcomes on your dice (be sure to name a kind of dice face but not a resources outcome).
After applying your dice powers and putting them back in your Inventory:

• Win 8 VP if only one out of the two faces matches the declared outcomes.
• Win 20 VP if both faces match the declared outcomes.
• Don’t win additional VP if neither matches the declared outcomes

Legion Atlantica is a Strategic level two player board-wargame set in an alternative history second century CE in which the Roman Empire discovered the New World 1300 years before Columbus. Game turns represent months, hexes are roughly twenty miles across and Strength Points correspond to approximately 500 men. The player who controls their opponent’s capital first or the player with the largest number of city points at the end of the game is the winner.

The Roman Player controls the Invading Roman forces attempting to establish “New Rome” and extend their control over nearby Mesoamerican city states. The Tikal player controls the principle Maya kingdom trying to stop them. Both sides may attempt to conquer or win the allegiance of other Mesoamerican city states that start the game neutral, these include small independent city states as well as larger regional powers.

The game features Leaders who command armies but are also needed to conduct negotiations. Roman fleets and Mesoamerican canoe transports, low levels of transatlantic Roman reinforcements, cities that can be fortified, pillaged or destroyed, slave workers, supply and attrition, cavalry (Roman player only) and human sacrifice (Tikal player only).

By necessity almost all of the forces and characters represented are fictional, however the map is a realistic representation of the actual Mesoamerican terrain and the city states are those that there is evidence for at this time. The game represents the importance of food and river transport to the Maya and the key influence of superstitions and festivals. Celestial markers for the Moon, Venus, Mars and Jupiter are moved on a night sky track causing increasing unrest or even rebellion and changes of allegiance amongst the regional powers and city states, especially when 2 or more markers stack together.

—description from the designer

description:
A game of precision, skill and silliness! See how steady your drawing hand is with a giant wobbly pen! In A Wobble is a laugh-out-loud drawing and guessing game designed to be played in teams, and is a guaranteed way to get the whole family involved in the fun. Great for parties or family occasions, In A Wobble challenges players to remain steady and draw specific items while their team mates guess what it is though the catch is that all this is done with a giant vibrating pen! So put your artistic skills to the test and see how you handle the wobbly pen, then enjoy the attempts of your friends and family in this fun game anyone can play. The set includes 1 x giant vibrating pen, 1 x paper pad, 25 x cards (featuring 150 x items to draw), 1 x sand timer and full playing instructions.

—description from the publisher

Summary:
Grendha is an epic miniature game told through the thrilling story of a God gone mad and his two son’s quest to restore order. To do so they need to collect all of the pages of the powerful book that once belonged to their father. However, they have different views on what to do with the book. Should they destroy it or should they use the power that its pages unlock?

Players: 2-4 players, Competitive and co-op experience.
Type: Exploration, Strategy, War, Area Control, Set Collection, Resource Management, Negotiating/Trade

Game Play

In Grendha you take on the helm of one of four powerful heroes.
There is an ancient tome, and it is said that the hidden secrets of creation were locked inside its pages. There are also whispers that the same book turned a once benevolent god mad and caused him to turn on his own people. There is no way to know if the powerful tome can be wielded for good and restore order because the pages were torn from the binding and scattered through time and space. The book could not be destroyed, but it could be hidden. It is time for the book to be restored.

The objective is to search the map, collect those pages, and reform the lost book Grendha. The game can be played in a purely competitive mode at two players, or a cooperative team play mode at four players.

At all player counts, each player will choose a hero and take command of them and their armies. During the game, you will explore the mythical lands, command your warriors, collect items, discover clues, and gather resources. Your hero will need to slip through time, enter the celestial city, and discover the location of the lost pages. Quick! Your hero must restore the book before it is too late.

How exciting to be on safari, trying to spot all your favourite animals... but, hang on, one of them has wandered off!
Be the first to spot the missing animal!

The double-sided cards feature two levels of play. Choose the side with 7 possible animals for children or for learning the game, and choose the other side with 12 possible animals for a challenge!

In every card there is one missing animal compared to the key cards showing all possible animals. There is no taking turns, you can take the card if you are the first player to identify that missing animal!

After one of the piles runs out, the game ends. The player that has collected most cards wins the game.

Fun and frantic card game of matching and observation.

Do you speak caveman? No? Well, here's your chance to learn!

There are four pictures in the cards, with four possible background colour. Distribute the cards evenly between players, and if everybody is ready the game begins!

There is no taking turns, you have to find a match as quick as possible! It's a rapid race to get rid of your cards before your megalithic mates!

For example: if a player laid a card and called out "mamu" (that means mammoth), and in his card played the mamu was with blue background, all players would search through their cards looking for a mamu with blue surround. Once a player finds this combination, he can place the card on top of the pile and can call out the next word! Shout out one of the caveman words so that everyone knows what to look for next...

The first player to get rid of all his cards wins the game!

The irreverence Blanc-Manger Coco now attacks the world of the fans of the imagination and digital culture. You like the Marvel saga and the hugs with our friend Pedobear, the sequences of "0" and "1" make you think of poetry more than arithmetic: La Geekerie is for you!

Mix the cards or not with the other games in the range: you decide.

Contains 160 reply cards and 40 question cards.

Description from the Back of the Box;

This expansion adds two new types of uniquely able Special Crew, the Academics and Merchants, and two new enemy types, the Acolyte Speaker and Acolyte Bloodletter, to the game, as well as new and unique Sea Event, Expedition and Denizen cards. An undertow of madness threatens to disturb what passes for peace in this world on the brink of annihilation...

18-card battle of mechs where the intial draft of equipment and weapons leaves two different mechs to use their tactical prowess to bring the other down.

Print-and-play card game based on the Battletech RPG. Tested, and created, by fans with attention to detail and balancing.

—description from the designer

Fifteen centuries have passed since Jesus descended from his cross, bathed Jerusalem in blood, and conquered Rome. The world has been ruled by inquisitors ever since.

Flame and Cross are symbols of The Holy Office, the institution whose sole purpose is to spread and defend the one and only true faith. But these symbols also contradict each other, for one represents Persian mages of fire, the other righteous Christians who fight them. The struggle goes on.

In this petty world, fear paralyzes the hearts of those who shy away from ruthless eradication of any signs of evil or corruption. This world is not even full of secrets, but it is surrounded by them, wallows in them, and cannot get away from them. Whenever an answer to a question is found, ten more questions appear immediately.

Inquisitor is a detective board game based on the series of best-selling short stories by Jacek Piekara that take you to the grim, fictional world of inquisitors.

Important note: Not any content of the game, neither images, nor the plot or background, is intended to offend anyone's religious feelings as they are merely props of a popular category known as "Religious Fiction".

This expansion adds a 6th available action to the game: Purchase Upgrade. This action allows players to spend collected resources to gain a permanent advantage for the duration of the game.

These are six additional cards which were added to the game for the 2018 reprint Kickstarter, and made available separately for owners of the first edition.

Zoo Animals is a memory game for 1-4 players, ages 2-6.

Each card in the deck features a name and picture of a different animal; there is one pair of cards for each animal.  Players place the cards face down, and take turns turning over two cards, trying to make a match.  Players who make a match collect the pair of cards and try again.  The game ends when all cards have been matched, and the winner is the player with the most cards.

The game also features a solvable puzzle on the back of the game cards.

—description from the publisher

Power to the Meeple is a complex, euro-styled strategy game where you attempt to become the mayor of Meepletown. The primaries have just ended and up to five candidates must fight to become the winner of the general election. Whether you choose to be a punk, a Democrat, a Republican and Independent or Green party is secondary to how well you organize your campaign and come up with a lean, effective strategy to win the most votes.

Power to the Meeple has three main gameplay phases. First the players participate in an augmented draft where they will be selecting resources. Most resources are just what they are, face value additions to a player's coffers. Alternatively, a player can pick lower valued resources that have abilities attached to them, which brings another balance to the player's strategy. Throughout the draft (which consists of as many rounds as players) each player is required to select one issue card which will stay with the player for the remainder of the game.

The second phase consists of each player, in turn order, deploying their five campaign tokens to the areas of the modular board they want to canvass (Urban, Estates, Eco-Buildings or Suburban areas).

During the third phase, tokens are revealed and the costs of the campaign are paid with the resources drafted earlier. Players who score in the top 3 positions on an area are usually awarded votes or other bonuses. Scores at individual locations are resolved by the key value comparisons printed on the areas, and the tie breakers are done according to who drafted the tie breakers such as Megaphone, Billboard, Scandal or Town Hall.

Once votes are counted, any money earned during that round are counted, players move up on their respective Urban, Suburban, Estate and Eco-Housing tracks, and lastly, discard all unused resources (besides money). All players also keep their issues.

Rounds 2 & 3 are similar to the first round, with the major difference being the advantages each player may have gained in the previous round.

After 3 rounds the players resolve any pending end of game awards or penalties for the last bit of votes and a winner is declared.

-description from designer

The Warhammer Armies: Kislev booklet describes the Kislev Allied Contingent army for the 6th Edition of Warhammer by Games Workshop. This 36 page long supplement was released through White Dwarf magazine.
(user summary)

North from the land of the Empire lies a land of dark pine forests and snow-clad wilderness - the land of the Kislev, ruled by mighty Tzars and Tzarinas. The icy realm of Kislev breeds tough and hardy people, not only because of the harsh climate, but also due to the constant depredations by raiders from the Chaos Wastes.

Warhammer Armies: Kislev is a compendium of White Dwarf articles for using Kislev allied contingents within an Empire army. This booklet describes the troops of Kislev, its history, its gods and its heroes.

Inside you will find:
- Kislevite Allied Contingents. New rules for using Kislevites, plus full background detail including the lands and people of Kislev, their history and their wars.

- The Ice Queen and Tzar Barons. Rules for your army to include both the mighty Boris Ursus, the red Tzar, upon his fearsome mount Urskin, and Tzarina Katarin, the Ice Queen of Kislev, wielder of the Ice Magic.
(from the back cover of the booklet)

This booklet was provided with White Dwarf 288.

A new book for Infinity. In it we can find all the details of the Third Offensive. Is Paradiso on the ropes? What’s the new threat to the Human Sphere? Enjoy the new rules, new Sectorial Armies and Fireteams.

This book covers the events that took place on the planet of Paradiso and makes clear that the alien threat is becoming very tangible and real. The Combined Army is not going to surrender, and Paradiso is only a bump on the road towards conquering the entire Human Sphere. In terms of where it lands within the Infinity narrative, it takes place directly after the events of Campaign Paradiso.

Paradiso lives up to its namesake: an astonishing paradise full of exquisite flora and fauna. In the past, this planet was inhabited by an alien race, though not much is known about the native sentient species. Their presence still lives on by the way of vast construction sites that can be found all over the planet, known as cosmolitos. It’s widely believed that these structures were the treasure that first caught the attention of the EI.

The high commanders of the various factions of the Human Sphere are trying to stop the EI from entering their systems by installing sophisticated blockades, but the Combined Army is managing to overcoming them with increasing frequency. In Third Offensive, you can read how they’re managing to smash through the different blockades and jeopardize the human hegemony on Paradiso.

The real objective of the EI will be also unveiled in this book.

What’s in the book?

As already stated, a lot of new background material that develops the narrative of the Infinity Universe, and it also provides several new locations to set the stage of our favorite wargame.

In addition to this, two new sectorials will be added to the roster of new releases - the unyielding Invincible Army of Yu-Jing and the Varuna Intervention Division for PanOceania. These will join the new armies most recently added with Operation Coldfront (Tartary Army Corps for Ariadna, and Operations Subsection for ALEPH)

Vancouver Buses is a roll and move game in which players take on the roles of bus drivers travelling around Vancouver. The first player to complete a circuit beginning and ending at the Kootenay Loop exchange wins.

The game was developed by the Vancouver Environment Education Project at the University of British Columbia to familiarise six to ten year old children with the Vancouver transit system.

—user summary

Jazzy Pips is a quick, cooperative dice game played in real-time for 3 to 6 players.

You’ve just formed a jazz band! It won’t be easy though. You know your instrument well, but you need to get to know the other musicians you’re playing with. This is the fun of joining a band! With practice, coordination, and the right tempo, you’ll be pulling off a memorable set at the local club in no time!

In Jazzy Pips, each player, or Musician,  must work together to overcome Song Cards by using your dice and making quick decisions at the same time. If you complete the Finale, you win! Be careful though, as the clock is ticking, and your dice are limited!

—description from the designer

Speedy Towers is a light, fun, tower-stacking dexterity game for the piecepack.

Players create towers by stacking tiles and coins on top of each other in real time. Build your tiles and coins the fastest to win.

—description from the designer

Reel Masters is a 1-8 player tabletop fishing game. Move your boat, choose your lure and cast your line in hopes of hooking the monsters! Once you hook a fish, roll your dice against the fish to determine who is stronger. Pull hard enough and you can land the fish and earn a chance at cash for powerful upgrades.

Reel Masters has 5 species of game fish commonly found in lakes throughout the United States. This unique game truly captures elements of the real life experience.

Compete in multiple game modes with teams, solo or co-op. If your boat has the most total weight at the end of the game you win! The board changes every time you play and with multiple ways to play, Reel Masters is sure to bring fun to the table.

Good Luck and Tight Lines!

—description from the publisher

Persist is a card game that helps women laugh at the ridiculous role assigned to them in the game of life. The double standards. Being told to smile by strangers. Mansplainers and manspreaders. Unsolicited questions about family planning. Not to mention all the ways women are doomed to fail at living up to culture’s standard of womanly virtue and grace. We know we sweat, we don’t glow.

Persist calls bullsh**. By asking women, their friends and allies to put all the cards out on the table—by being real, Persist invites women to laugh, learn from each other, connect meaningfully… and get up to fight another day.

How to Play Persist: Be the most persistent by earning three cards of the same life value (career, health, leisure, or relationships) to achieve a milestone. Collect three milestones to win.

Cards fall under six categories of gameplay: truth, would you rather, trivia, sharing, roadblocks, act it out, and the occasional wild card. Each category requires a different kind of action for the player to perform individually, with others, or even the whole group. Once played your cards may not be safe! Other players may steal your cards if they are going after the same values. This is where things get fun!

—description from the designer

Goblin Derby is a racing game for 2-4 players. The first player to finish 3 laps around the dangerous and haphazard track is the winner.

Before the race starts players must first scavenge parts from the junkyard to build their, often wacky, vehicle. Then, after assembling their monstrosity, players roll dice to move their cart along the track.

Watch out for the other players, they will try anything to win from ramming you to throwing rocks to shooting rocket propelled goblins.

—description from the publisher

Players take the role of businessmen during the 14th through 16th century in coastal Europe.  They can explore the seas and new lands, establish trade routes with foreign villages, gain influence in government through bribes, fight naval battles, trade shares of stock in maritime and colonial companies, engage in piracy, and invest in government and company bonds. They will also compete in score with 3 NPC factions: the mainland Europeans, the moneylenders, and the natives.

SHARES OF STOCK
The stock market system is pretty standard. Companies have 10 shares each and the player that owns the most shares controls the company. However, share price is not impacted by buys and sells, but only value of the company. One key difference in this to most stock market games is that not only can players own shares of stock in companies, but other companies and governments can own shares of stock as well. For example, if a government owns majority of shares in a company the player that controls that government then manages the company. A key difference compared to most other shareholding games is that the company can require shareholders to contribute further funds if the company is low on money. This was often the case in the early days of shareholding.

COMPANIES
Companies can build trading vessels that allow them to explore the sea and try to find overseas villages to trade with. They can also buy bonds released by governments or other companies. They can also build factories (old term for trading posts) in newly discovered lands as well as explore land. Companies do not start with any money, so they need to raise investments from players, governments, and other companies (or borrow from the moneylenders).

PORT CITIES (GOVERNMENTS)
Port cities are controlled by a magistrate. Players can become magistrates by bribing to gain prestige in the port city. Port cities are able to construct warships. Magistrates can also decide which companies can build vessels in their port city, as well as determine any tax on incoming trade brought by trading vessels. Magistrates can also ban the moneylenders to forgive all debt related to the magistrate, but this will make the moneylenders unavailable for the rest of the game to them.

EXPLORATION
The map is almost completely undiscovered, and as players move their watercraft into unknown territory 1 of 3 things can happen, 1: they discover empty water 2: they discover land, sometimes with a native village and sometimes with out 3: they run encounter a storm and sink. As all this happens map tiles are placed.

MONEYLENDERS
The moneylenders are the international banking community and are extremely important in the game. Players and governments start out with very limited funds, so the moneylenders are almost always needed. This mechanism is unique, and different than almost all other banking systems in games. The moneylenders themselves have limited funds, but practice fractional reserve banking. This means they lend out more money than they have, so it is possible that they will go under if other entities are unable or refuse to pay debts.

COMBAT
Combat in the game is simple and based on dice rolls. Players can go to war with each other and the natives. Cargo and other watercraft can also sometimes be seized.

BONDS
Governments and companies can issue bonds to raise funds. Companies, players, and governments can invest in these.

MAINLAND POWER
At the beginning of each turn the mainland power may attack a random port city. The mainland power represents mainland Europe. Tribute can be paid to avoid a possible attack. The mainland power may become powerful, but can also be reduced to nothing.

TRADE
The trading mechanism is simple, and is the main way to generate wealth. Company vessels transfer numbers that represent cargo back and forth between overseas villages and port cities.

WINNING THE GAME
The final score is based on the wealth of the player's family at the end of the game. The score of the mainland power, the moneylenders, the natives, and all ports and companies are also included. Players must attempt to get as much wealth in the hands of their family as possible, because at the end of the game the companies and ports they manage to contribute to their own score.

SOLO GAME
The solo game is the same as the normal game. It is able to function as so because of the NPC entities that have a final score, it is not meant for serious gaming. Its main purpose is to get a player accustomed to the peculiarities of the game.

Sjónarspil is an Icelandic family and party game. Players get cards with adjectives and must choose the cards that best fit their opponents. Who in the group is the nerd and who is the helpful one? boardgame: Pour Taste: The spin it, mix 'em, drink up game!
description:

The spin will tell the player which ingredients to mix up -- or whether to choose another player to drink, steal chips, or go bankrupt. Will the drink taste ok or totally gross? It's up to the player to decide whether or not they will drink up. Every time a player drinks from a cup, they earn a chip. If they decide not to drink the concoction, they lose a chip. Nemesis 9 Tyrantis has on more than one occasion been mistaken for the leader of the planet’s Genestealer Cult infestation – however, so long as the Broodlord gets to feed on the minds and bodies of the populace, it cares little for the chatterings of ambulatory biomass.

A 2 player chess derivative type game where players roll dice and set up Fortifications to gain control of and dominate the battlefield. Players will be able to obtain Supplies to aid their endeavors as they both vie for the top.

Each player controls one of four Armies which each consist of 7 different pieces (each Army differs in type) that can be used for tactical thinking, critical math skills for younger children, and a simple-but-fun and straightforward gameplay.

Suitable for ages as low as 8+, and the game comes with:

     14 polyhedral dice
     10 colored six-sided dice
     2 white six-sided dice
     9 Supply sticks
     sheet of felt
     rule book
     piece of merchandise
     game map


Players take turns moving their Armies across the board and attacking each other and each other's Fortifications as they vie for last man standing status. Player polyhedral dice start on their highest numbers and are reduced through battle until they are wiped from the board. Fortifications include: Outposts, Bases, and Forts, each with differing ranges and providing different advantages/disadvantages. Supplies include: Lapis Slabs, Hemp Stacks, Blood Vials, Gold Ingots, and Lavender Sprigs.

Can be played on the provided game map, or on any chess/draught board. Complex enough for adults to lose themselves playing for hours while still simple enough to appeal to children. Makes a perfect gift for the board gamer friend/significant other in your life!

While sea and air combat are critical, the only way to drive an opponent from a territory is through the use of ground forces.

17-Steps is an awareness-raising game. It’s to raise an awareness of the SDGs, there are 17 of them. You can’t talk about achieving the global goals if you don’t first create awareness about it. Atnor (means ‘five’ in Eggon Language) is a game of five. Players are imersed in a tactical environment to have a set of five. Perfect for lovers of logic and strategy.
Gbosi means ‘Symbol of Authority’. It is a game that tells the true story of the fight for a throne between two men. Who gets the throne? Play to find out.

Build your Nigerian home regardless of tribe or religion and be the first to do that if you want to win. The story behind Homia is that Nigeria is a multi-cultural, multi-ethnic and multi-religious country and the player must create a family regardless of the culture or religion. It’s designed to be easy to play. You choose a family and build the family.

Homia is a Memory-style game, where you look at a face down card and try to remember what kind it is. Until you find a homestead tile (also called a "family pad"), you will only memorize where the various tiles are, and put them back face down on the table. Once you finally discover a family pad, you take it into your area. The family pad has various symbols that represent different parts of a household, such as fathers, mothers, sisters, brothers, and pets. The color of the symbol tells you what kind of other cards you will need to complete your family pad. If your family pad has a green symbol, for example, you need to collect one "father" (green background) card to add to your family pad. If there are three yellow symbols on the family pad card, it means he has three daughters, so you'll need to find three of the "daughter" (yellow background) cards to complete your family. If you have a good memory of the cards you looked at before finding your family pad, it will be much easier to find the members of the household required to complete your family pad. Whoever completes their family pad first is the winner!

LUKU-LUKU is an education game with three editions – Noun, Abstract and Map. It’s a fast game and based on the name, it is about how fast your eyes can move. This game tends to make people aware of their surroundings. Lukuluku Map is more of a geographical game while Abstract teaches stars, numbers
and regular shapes in a fun way depending on what the player wants to learn.

Mgbako (means ‘to calculate’ in Igbo language) is a speed arithetic Boardgame made in Nigeria. Players compete to earn points by beating their opponent in simple arithmetic. It’s not as simple as it sounds. Try winning when the stakes are up and tensions are high.

It’s a folklore of the Igbo culture meaning Proud Woman. Players compete as
soothsayers to save her from her untimely demise.
Oya is a game designed based on the life of a Lagosian (someone who lives in Lagos Nigeria). It explores the Olokpa (police), the JJCs, The Area Boys and hustlers alike. It’s a fast-paced game.

Safe Journi chronicles the journey of a traveller on a Nigerian road. Your fellow players use the obstacles encountered in a Nigerian road to prevent you from reaching your destination. Your task is to manoeuvre the obstacles and get to your destination unharmed.

The Berzuline conference did not go as planned. Tempers are high, fresh wounds come to mind. The treasure lay awaiting exploitation.

It was clear a battle would occur.

UKUBUWA is a misspelt Swahili word for ‘Greatness’, and it takes you through a journey of young people as you earn and lose wealth. , Ukubuwa is a game that takes you through the four stages of life in your perspective.
It starts with the Learning stage (things that affect you in this stage), then takes you to the Idea stage (Talent Development), the Philanthropy level. The interesting thing is that it brings choice and chance to play – chance because you have to roll dice and play, choice because you have to make decisions. It was designed basically for storytelling. While playing, you gain certain things like how to negotiate, how to spend money. It’s the mechanism. Overall, it’s a game of choice and chance.

The rules themselves can be tailored to suit any fantasy setting and the game system is designed to allow you to create and field any troops that you like, limited only by your imagination and figures available. A comprehensive points system allows the design of troops as you think they should be represented.

boardgame: Tanks & Yanks: World War I 1917-1918 – Deluxe Edition
description:
It is now 2017, one hundred years since the United States entered the First World War on the side of the Allies in one of the most unnecessary and wasteful wars ever fought.

The research required almost got in the way of the game system, with diagrams and illustrations. Consequently after system, with diagrams and illustrations. Consequently after many rewrites it was decided to create one book as a game system and another as a simple note book. Both over lap each other. It did seem necessary to describe the tactical techniques in some great detail. Fortunately there is plenty of information on how these weapons were used on the battlefield. This availability of information made it possible to transform information into a realistic game system. However incomplete as it may be, the rules still effectively cover only a small part of the late ground war.

The game system includes supportive diagrams, illustrations, tactical notes and Quick Reference Sheets (Q.R.S.). The Turn Sequence and Turn Phases, once understood, make play easy and fast. The system has two Turn Sequences one of which is optional and the other mandatory. Six basic Turn Phases move the game along to a conclusion. Manufacturers will be glad by the fact that the game is best played using lots of figures and models. Let us be glad that there are sources for figures and models.

Basic units are 12 figures for a platoon with four platoons making a company and three to four companies making a battalion. Figures can be mounted individually or on multiple stands. Morale is based on unit size and applicable modifiers. Movement is with a D-10 die roll for both infantry and tanks. Transport and field artillery movement is determined by the roll of a D-6. There are two Turn Phases in which players can shoot. Hand-to-hand combat is easily performed. Players are always encouraged to adjust the rules to suit their perceptions.

To me, Yanks and Tanks captures the flavor of the late war. I hope your games will be hard fought, yet fun, and the game system competes well with other similar systems.

—description from designer

KOKAGNE 357 is an abstract Domino style tile-laying game for 3 - 6 players.

The goal of the game is to be the first to get rid of all his Kominos.

Players begin the game with 5 Kominos. On his turn, each player places 1 Komino on the table and draws a new one at the end of his turn. Each Komino that is placed on the table (with the exception of the first one) must be orthogonally in contact with at least 3 symbols of previously played Kominos and at least 2 symbols must match. If a player completes an orthogonal or diagonal row with 3, 5, or 7 symbols of the same color/shape, he is allowed to give 1, 2 or 3 Kominos to his opponents.

-description from designer

A card game about haemophilia that allows the player to experience what it means to live with this inherited genetic disorder that impairs the body's ability to make blood clots, a process needed to stop bleeding.

This playing game can be played solo or with multiple players. Goal of the game is to score as many points as possible by doing all sorts of activities. More risky activities increase the risk of hemorrhages, which need to be avoided as much as possible.

Politics can be dirty and politics can be frustrating but now politics can be fun with this fast-paced exciting card game. Players work to win the nomination of their party in the first phase of play then join together in teams to compete in the national election. Votes are gained by using political influence in this trick-taking game. Do you have what it takes to run for office and lead your country to greatness or, at least, have a lot of fun trying?

Run For Office is played in two phases of seven rounds each. The first phase is the Primary where each player works to win the nomination of their own party. The second phase is the Election where players work together in two competing teams to elect a president (winner).

In the Primary, each player gets a party, negotiates a platform by trading cards, and then gains Influence Cards. The Influence Cards are used to win Vote Cards by taking tricks. The Top two players who collect the most votes in their party are the winners of the Primary. The remaining players form teams with the two winners and a final Election takes place. Again, players gain Influence Cards to take Vote Cards through tricks but must do so in teams. The party members with the most votes at the end of the Election are the winners.

-description from designer

description:

The aim of the game is to be elected President by the majority. This means getting the necessary votes to win more than half of the seats in the National Congress.

In order to get the votes, everything is allowed. Deals with other political parties, buying judges, cheating... but you also need to take into account the other political parties and luck!

You can also choose your lifestyle and what proposals you are going to invest the money from your campaign into. Also you need to decide what you are going to do during your time as President to ensure you win the elections for the following year.

Each turn, players will roll the dice and move their token on the track - either clockwise or counter-clockwise. They then draw a card from a pile corresponding to the colour of the space they landed on. Each colour has its own actions associated with it: Green for political expertise, black for family life, purple for business, gold for bribe and deals.

During the game, several elections are held. During each elections, players invest money - which buys them votes. These translated in positions in congress, which will give back money. There are also special positions, which will give you different advantages. A player wins the game if he manages to get a majority during the elections (50%+1).

Players can negotiate alliances, which allows them to share the benefits of special positions - though it is not possible to win together.

Take a thrilling ride through Sacramentos most beautiful and iconic locations, while collaborating to connect them together and with the game.

- A great filler or gateway game that will hit the table often.
- The game can be taught in a few minutes and is highly thematic and accessible.
- Bike route is a light route building game that is semi-cooperative but includes optional rules for more complexity.
- The game has a great climax where eveybody fells like they are 'one turn away from winning.

In the game players build bike routes around the city winning community rewards. The first player to 7 points wins.

The game includes exquisite paintings of local landmarks and locations both past, present and future. This is not the Sacramento of today but a Sacramento of the imagination, with ideas that excite the spirit, from the historical past to the hopeful future. Bike Route includes everything from the majestic Alhambra movie palace to the soon to be completed Powerhouse Science Center on the river. All the wonderful things that you think about when you imagine Sacramento are included. Come play in the Sacramento that never was but will always exist in our hearts.

Bike Route has a long history and has been in development for over 10 years. Originally called Connections the game was never published and looked like it would end up on the pile of great ideas that never were. My daughter AR kept it alive by insisting that we play. "Papa lets play Connections" she would say and we would play. Despite the weak art the game was good and we kept enjoying the drama of the game. Eventually we decided that it should be brought back with new artwork and Bike Route was born. Connections was at heart a game about building connections between places and it seemed a perfect fit for a game about building bike routes. The idea took off and it has kept going ever since.

I was play-testing Bike Route at the main library with a local family and the boy across from me said "We live here..." and pointed at the map. "and I have been to the Zoo." he continued, indicating Land Park at the bottom of the board. Watching him I realized that a lot of kids know places in Sacramento but their mental map doesn't really connect them together. It was great to see the gears turning in his head as he saw all the places in town fit. He also knew DOCO and Old Town and quite a few other places. This was moment of affirmation; I realized that Bike Route could also bring back that great sense of place that is getting lost. Google guides us from place to place without any context or sense of relationship. Yes you can turn left and turn right but where are you?

I travel a lot for work and I am always happy to come home. Ususally I go to a town that is excited about what they are doing but in Sac we have it in spades. It seems that every week brings something new to our doorstep. Mabye a brand new artist or shome classy hippy selling tea. We have to recognize this exciting moment we are living in and celebrate it. Bike Route puts this fantasitc idea down on paper and freezes this instant so we can stop and savor what we have. Take a break from the hustle and bustle and enjoy a great game with family and friends. Recognize how lucky we are and enjoy it.

Bike route is for people who love bikes, games and Sacramento.

Overlap is a card game using small, square cards, each with nine squares that are each colored red, yellow, or blue. The goal of the game is to score points by placing cards such that squares of the same color align. As the name suggests, the cards can overlap one another, adding another level of strategy and requiring spatial-reasoning and critical thinking skills.

How to play:

Set up: Shuffle the deck of cards, then deal each player five cards. Each player places this stack of cards face down in front of them. Place one card from the deck face up in the center of the playing surface.

Gameplay:  Choose a starting player. That player draws the top card from his/her stack and plays it on the playing surface following card placement rules, and then calculates and records his/her score for the turn. Play then continues clockwise. When all players have placed all their cards, the round ends and the cards are shuffled back into the deck. The number of rounds corresponds with the number of players, with each player having an opportunity to go first.

Card placement: Cards may be placed adjacent to other cards in play, but they must be played so that at least one square aligns with a square on an adjacent card. Additionally, they may be played so that the cards partially overlap, as long as the squares align. When cards are played, at least one square must lie over an empty space (you cannot occlude 9 squares with any given card.)

Scoring: When you place a card, any instance in which a square of a certain color lies next to a square of that same color on another card is worth one point. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins!

-description from designer

boardgame: Adventure Post: A Path of Self-Learning
description:
Adventure Post is a series of solo-mini-adventures delivered to your door on a postcard each week. Every week is a small portion of an overall larger campaign which will take many, many weeks to come to conclusion. The postcard will include a QR code or website address that will direct you to the weekly adventure document. The document will explain the setup, instructions, and key areas on the adventure map.

A Path of Self-Learning uses simplistic character creation and dice rolls for combat and interacting with the world. There are a series of questions, all of which alter the final conflict of the game in various ways. On the 12th week, the adventure will be completed and to survive is to win.

The Story:
As punishment for a crime committed against humanity, the Lord of the land has sentenced you to a Trial of Character. You must be judged against both physical and mental challenges in which you will discover who you truly are. You will learn about the three principles, Truth, Love, and Courage. You will face moral dilemmas in each of the eight virtues where your answers must be considered carefully. Finally, you will face a mighty foe in one-on-one combat. A foe whose power is a reflection of the choices you have made. Can you prove yourself worthy to earn your mantle back, Avatar?

A Path of Self-Learning is only the first adventure in a larger overall campaign. There are more aventures of different themes and new rules on the horizon.

—description from the publisher

description:
Warren Wars is a game about racing to be the first to build your army while your opponents are trying to steal your Carrots and bunnies.

Each player takes turns first drawing a card and then playing a card. Everyone is aiming to be the winner by being the first to fill their Warren with five Recruits or by being the last player standing after wiping out everyone else.

There will be card stealing, taking over other players’ turns, all while trying to maintain their own Warrens.

—description from the publisher

Psychobunnies – What’s that then?

It’s an incredibly addictive tabletop card game that challenges your memory & strategy skills whilst still having an element of luck that can come around to bite you on the backside when you least expect it. Oh, and it’s got psychotic bunnies. Don’t forget the bunnies.

To win the game, you need to ensure you have the lowest score possible each round. The last bunny standing is the winner. That might sound easy, but with only 10 seconds to view half or your drawn cards and the other half of your hand a complete mystery, it soon becomes more of a challenge!

Play can become incredibly strategic (e.g. do you 'play safe' and try to attain low scoring cards, or do you collect high scoring cards that others are more likely to discard, running the risk of being stuck with them when play ends?).

Then there's the element of luck. You have no idea what half of your deck contains. Sometimes at the end of a round, they're as much as a surprise to you as to the rest of the players. This can be a good or a bad thing! Add to that several 'Trick or Treat' cards designed to test your skills (and your sanity), and Psychobunnies is an absolute riot to play.

No bunnies were harmed in the making of this game.

—description from the designer
